Overview:

Our client functions as the IT team for their customers. They will be hiring a Cloud Administrator 1 with strong PowerShell scripting or coding skills and strong Microsoft 365 skills.

This role is responsible for implementing, configuring and supporting technology solutions throughout the organization with a focus on Azure and Microsoft 365 solutions.

In the first 90 days, the expectation is to work primarily onsite in Vancouver, WA. Once you get up to speed, 2-3 days remote expectation is ideal.

The target customers are small businesses (100 employees or less) in the northwest, every customer is word of mouth, leadership really cares about customers feeling like they've got an IT partner they can trust.

Selling points:

A candidate who enjoys being an IT generalist tend to be the best fit. They are not bleeding edge with technology, but everyone makes an impact on the company, you'll get to influence how the company evolves and directly help small businesses in the northwest. The work culture values structure and documentation, they are 100% ticket-driven organization.

Responsibilities

Manage existing technology solutions to ensure appropriate optimal performance, availability, and patchingAnalyze, log, track and resolve complex software/hardware issues pertaining to TCP/IP networks, printers, servers, and applications to meet business needsPerform troubleshooting as required, as such, lead problem-solving efforts often involving outside vendors and other support personnel and/or organizationsResponsible for the development of current state documentation and knowledge base articles; Update documents regularly, and maintain accurate documentation on system performance and operational activitiesCreate functional-need scripts to ease and simplify administrationAdhere to and promote existing ITIL processes and standard procedures as documentedDocument standard configurations for administered systemsFollow Change Management practices Qualifications2 years of experience creating Microsoft 365 and Cloud infrastructure solutions (Azure AD or Entra ID)Strong PowerShell scripting or coding skills.Experience managing Active Directory (create and deploy GPOs, user/computer groups, manage organization permissions strategies) Preferred QualificationsMicrosoft Azure / 365 certificationsKnowledge of network topology, firewalls, routers and wired/wireless infrastructuresExperience with Microsoft SharePoint administration Our TalentAffirm process: All applicants are personally reviewed by a recruiter. Candidates who complete our TalentAffirm screen and assessment are prioritized for next steps. It's an opportunity for you to stand out above your competition.

jobs.title: Engineer II(Windows and MacOS Endpoint Engineer)
jobs.summary: Job Description : We are seeking highly skilled and motivated Windows and MacOS Endpoint Engineer II to join our dynamic IT team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing and maintaining endpoint devices, ensuring their optimal performance,...
jobs.description:
Job Description :

We are seeking highly skilled and motivated Windows and MacOS Endpoint Engineer II to join our dynamic IT team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing and maintaining endpoint devices, ensuring their optimal performance, security, and compliance with organizational standards. This role requires a deep understanding of both Windows and MacOS environments and management tools, as well as the ability to troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues. In this role, you will help to build a modern desktop platform for our end users.

Education and Experience
Required:

Undergraduate degree in engineering or computer science discipline. 5 years or more work experience in an IT endpoint engineering role in leu of a degree. Experience in information technology includes the following: Experience designing and developing Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM), Intune, and Jamf Pro solutions for application distribution, configuration management, asset management, and other endpoint lifecycle capabilities following the least privilege security model. Scripting experience with a focus on automating management of windows end points and user data migration to new computers. Experience designing, testing, and implementing configuration, compliance, and conditional access policies withing Azure. Experience designing, testing, and implementing Active Directory and or azure group policies. Experience designing, testing, and implementing Virtual Desktop solutions. Familiarity with endpoint security best practices, including antivirus, encryption, and patch management. Excellent verbal and written communication skills for a wide range of audiences including executives, business stakeholders and IT teams. Strong attention to detail with an ability to operate effectively across multiple priorities. High degree of adaptability that keeps pace with rapid changes in IT technology.

Preferred:

Demonstrated experience with bringing in state of the art endpoint management capabilities into a large, geographically distributed environment.

Demonstrated experience architecting and engineering deployment workflows using JAMF and Microsoft Autopilot

Proficiency in scripting languages such as PowerShell, Bash, or Python for automation tasks.

Relevant certifications such as Microsoft Certified: Modern Desktop Administrator Associate, Jamf Certified Tech, or similar are a plus

CORE WORK ACTIVITIES

Provides support, oversight, governance, and tactical direction that are required to enable the delivery of end user IT services. Provide significant input to the overall end user infrastructure architecture and operational governance model. Researching, designing, and implementing components that are standards based, high performing, highly available, and secure in delivering the required business functionality to end users. Provide operational guidance to the enterprise on the endpoint management platforms. Provides assessments of the current environment to detect critical deficiencies and recommends solutions for improvement. Utilizes capability modeling to align systems strategy and planning with business strategy and goals. Consults with project teams to identify when it is necessary to modify services to accommodate project needs. Implements and promotes standard configuration and change management, processes, and practices. Develop test plans, implementation plans, and project timelines for assigned projects. Provide work assignments and mentorship to a team of user infrastructure engineers using industry established best practices. Provides routine status reporting of compliance and health of end user endpoints.

Delivering Technology:

Performs quantitative and qualitative analyses for service delivery processes and projects. Supports existing systems and projects in a minimum of one environment. Reviews completeness of requirements prior to solutioning. Plans and coordinates implementation, reviews quality control of systems functional design, usability, functionality, and implementation. Coordinates with appropriate IT and vendor relations teams. Ensures early warning to the leadership regarding degraded or missed service levels.

IT Governance:

Implement and utilize automation to discover, manage, and remediate endpoints to include BIOS settings, BIOS and Driver upgrades, software patches and other configurable items to effectively manage and assure compliance of the end user endpoints. Develop dashboard constructs to be implemented to provide insights into compliance, status, and a real-time overview of end-user device key performance indicators (KPIs) or data points. Develop and maintain detailed process and control documentation.

Primary Responsibilities:

maintenance of CrowdStrike agents to ensure full coverage across all endpoints.Incident Response: Lead and participate in the investigation and response to security incidents detected by CrowdStrike cloud-hosted SaaS Solution, to manage policies, control reporting data, manage, and respond to threats. Experience in backend solution, day to day system admin and monitoring, file system management and disk management and creation of shell scripts for the automation of tasks and the extraction of logsProvide follow-up reports (technical findings, feedback, resolution steps taken) for Root Cause analysis, engineering technical assessment and process improvement initiativesBasic Qualifications:

BS degree in Science, Technology, Engineering or related field and 8-10 years of prior relevant experience with a focus on cybersecurity OR Masters with 6-8 years of prior relevant experience.Experience leading cross-functional security initiatives and managing small teams or projects.Extensive, hands-on experience with the CrowdStrike Falcon platform, including multiple modules (e.g., EDR, FIM, Identity Protection, Data Protection).Expertise in endpoint security concepts and incident response processes.Proficiency in scripting languages like Python, PowerShell, or Bash for automation and custom response actions.Strong knowledge of security frameworks like NIST or MITRE ATT&CK.Experience with SIEM integrations and threat analysis.Solid understanding of operating systems (Windows, Linux, macOS).Superior attention to detail with excellent written and verbal communication skills.Excellent problem solving, research, and follow-up skills.Self-motivation and ability to work independently.Ability to obtain an Entry of Duty (EOD) Public Trust.Candidate must, at a minimum, meet one of these certifications: CrowdStrike certifications (e.g., CCFR, CCFP).Preferred Qualifications:

Experience automating management tasks with Ansible, Puppet, or ChefExperience working with SIEM tools such as Splunk to ingest, normalize, store, and maintain data from endpoint, network, and application sourcetypes.Experience working in AWS and AzureExperience with Wiz, Sepio, TVM and Nucleus is a plusAt Leidos, we don’t want someone who "fits the mold"—we want someone who melts it down and builds something better. This is a role for the restless, the over-caffeinated, the ones who ask, “what’s next?” before the dust settles on “what’s now.”

jobs.title: Senior Microsoft Endpoint Management Engineer
jobs.summary: The Senior Microsoft Endpoint Management Engineer is responsible for designing, implementing, maintaining, and supporting enterprise endpoint management solutions using both Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM/SCCM) and Microsoft Intune. This...
jobs.description:
The Senior Microsoft Endpoint Management Engineer is responsible for designing, implementing, maintaining, and supporting enterprise endpoint management solutions using both Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM/SCCM) and Microsoft Intune.

This role serves as a subject matter expert for traditional and modern endpoint management, including operating system deployment, application lifecycle management, device compliance, security controls, endpoint analytics, and cloud-based device management.

The ideal candidate possesses deep expertise in both MECM and Intune and can help organizations modernize endpoint management through co-management, Microsoft Entra ID integration, Windows Autopilot, and cloud-native management strategies.

Key Responsibilities

Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM / SCCM)

Design, implement, administer, and maintain MECM infrastructure, including site systems, boundaries, boundary groups, distribution points, and client settings.
Monitor MECM health, performance, and availability; perform upgrades, hotfix installations, and environment maintenance.
Design and maintain Windows 10/11 operating system deployment task sequences for bare-metal, refresh, and in-place upgrade scenarios.
Manage boot images, driver repositories, deployment media, and PXE/task sequence troubleshooting.
Package, test, deploy, and maintain enterprise applications using MSI, EXE, PowerShell, scripts, and establish detection methods, dependencies, supersedence, and deployment requirements.
Manage Windows and third-party patching solutions, including deployment rings, maintenance windows, compliance monitoring, and remediation.
Microsoft Intune / Cloud Endpoint Management

Design, implement, and administer Microsoft Intune environments for Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android device management.
Configure device enrollment, Microsoft Entra ID join, hybrid join, compliance policies, configuration profiles, device restrictions, and endpoint security policies.
Design and implement Windows Autopilot scenarios, including user-driven, pre-provisioned, and self-deploying deployments.
Configure Enrollment Status Page behavior, Autopilot profile assignments, and troubleshoot enrollment and provisioning issues.
Package and deploy Win32, Microsoft Store, Microsoft 365 Apps, and line-of-business applications; manage application lifecycle and update processes.
Configure and maintain security baselines, BitLocker management, Endpoint Privilege Management, Microsoft Defender integration, and Conditional Access integration.
Co-Management & Endpoint Modernization

Design and implement MECM/Intune co-management solutions and migrate workloads between MECM and Intune.
Develop endpoint modernization roadmaps that guide customers from traditional device management to cloud-native management.
Assess customer endpoint environments and recommend best-practice architectures for modern management, compliance, and security.
Lead technical workshops, design sessions, implementation efforts, and customer-facing endpoint management projects.
Automation, Documentation & Reporting

Develop PowerShell scripts for endpoint automation, administration, reporting, and remediation.
Integrate endpoint management workflows with Microsoft Graph API where applicable.
Create and maintain technical documentation, SOPs, runbooks, architecture diagrams, and implementation guides.
Develop reports using MECM, Intune, Power BI, SQL, and Microsoft reporting tools to track deployment success, compliance, and endpoint metrics.
Provide Tier 3 escalation support and mentor junior engineers or support staff.
Required Qualifications

5+ years of hands-on experience administering MECM/SCCM in enterprise environments.
5+ years of hands-on experience administering Microsoft Intune.
Strong experience with Windows 10 and Windows 11 deployment, management, and troubleshooting.
Experience with operating system deployment, application packaging/deployment, patch management, endpoint security, and device compliance.
Strong understanding of Microsoft Entra ID, Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, DHCP, and PKI/certificates.
Advanced PowerShell scripting and automation experience.
Experience implementing MECM and Intune co-management.
Strong troubleshooting, communication, documentation, and customer-facing consulting skills.
Preferred Qualifications

Experience with Microsoft Defender for Endpoint, Defender XDR, Microsoft Cloud PKI, Endpoint Privilege Management, and Microsoft security baselines.
Experience with Microsoft Graph API, Power BI reporting, tenant-to-tenant migrations, endpoint modernization projects, and third-party patching solutions.
Experience supporting SMB, education, healthcare, government, or enterprise organizations.
Ability to lead discovery sessions, translate technical needs into project scopes, and present recommendations to customers and internal stakeholders.
Preferred Certifications

Strongly Preferred

MD-102: Endpoint Administrator Associate

MS-102: Microsoft 365 Administrator Expert

Additional

SC-300: Identity and Access Administrator

AZ-104: Azure Administrator Associate

Ideal Candidate

The ideal candidate has extensive experience managing both MECM and Intune environments and understands how to bridge traditional endpoint management with modern cloud-based management. They can independently lead customer implementations, support large-scale device deployments, automate administrative processes, and provide strategic guidance around endpoint modernization and endpoint security.

This is a senior technical position requiring strong consulting skills, customer engagement experience, and the ability to lead endpoint management projects from design through implementation and support.

GApZY15867

jobs.title: Endpoint Administrator - Microsoft Intune
jobs.summary: Dexian has an immediate opportunity available for a detail-oriented and motivated Endpoint Administrator with MS Intune experience to manage, maintain, and optimize a unified endpoint management environment for our premier aerospace customer. The Endpoint...
jobs.description:
Dexian has an immediate opportunity available for a detail-oriented and motivated Endpoint Administrator with MS Intune experience to manage, maintain, and optimize a unified endpoint management environment for our premier aerospace customer. The Endpoint Administrator will be the primary point of contact for the deployment, configuration, compliance, and security of all corporate-owned and BYOD (Bring Your Own Device) endpoints. Utilizing the Microsoft 365 stack-specifically Microsoft Intune and Microsoft Entra ID-you will ensure seamless, secure hardware and software lifecycles across Windows and iOS platforms.

A typical workday may consist of the following duties as directed by the client:

Policy & Profile Management: Build, test, and deploy Intune configuration profiles, compliance policies, and security baselines to standardize client environments.Application Packaging & Deployment: Package, test, and distribute internal and third-party software updates, web links, and store apps across the enterprise.Modern Provisioning: Administer and monitor modern device deployment systems, such as Windows Autopilot and Apple Business Manager, ensuring a zero-touch or minimal-touch provisioning pipeline for new hires.Identity & Access Control: Collaborate with identity teams to manage device registration, primary user changes, group memberships, and targeted Conditional Access enforcement.Troubleshooting & Escalations: Act as the Tier 2/3 escalation point for device enrollment failures, application installation blocks, and profile synchronization issues.Reporting & Governance: Monitor Intune reporting dashboards daily to identify non-compliant devices, update patch status deficiencies, and remediate systemic configuration drift.Documentation: Maintain clear, concise documentation outlining deployment processes, app packaging standards, and device lifecycle flows.
REQUIREMENTS AND SKILLS:
To be successful in this role, you should possess the following skills:

Security Stack Exposure: Familiarity integrating Intune with security tools like Microsoft Defender for Endpoint or implementing data protection via MAM (Mobile Application Management) without full device enrollment.Problem-Solving & Analytical Rigor: A structured, logical approach to debugging deployment failures, policy conflicts, and log file analysis.Cross-Functional Communication: The ability to explain technical enrollment steps or security restrictions clearly and empathetically to non-technical business professionals.Adaptability: Curiosity and willingness to stay current with Microsoft's continuous cloud release notes, feature changes, and best-practice evolutions.
To be eligible for this position, you must meet or exceed each of the following:

Experience: 6-8 years of progressive experience in IT systems administration, with a minimum of 2-3 years dedicated specifically to managing and deploying policies within Microsoft Intune (Cloud-only or Hybrid/co-management configurations).Technical Ecosystem Knowledge: Solid foundation in Microsoft 365 workloads, including Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory), Conditional Access policies, and Group Policy Objects (GPOs).OS Support: Demonstrated experience configuring, enrolling, and troubleshooting Windows 10/11 endpoints. Basic exposure to managing mobile operating systems (iOS) via MDM (Mobile Device Management) profiles.Scripting Essentials: Basic proficiency with PowerShell for writing, editing, and deploying automated scripts or application wrappers to endpoints.Core Infrastructure: Solid understanding of core networking and identity concepts, including TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, and modern identity provisioning.
This position requires an active Department of Defense security clearance at the SECRET level or ability to obtain and maintain this clearance.
U.S. citizens ONLY are eligible for a security clearance and this position.

LOCATION:

This is an on-site position and the successful candidate may be based in one of the following locations: El Segundo, CA, Chantilly, VA or Colorado Springs, CO.
EDUCATION and CERTIFICATIONS:

Associate's degree or higher requiredPossession of or progress toward the Microsoft Certified: Endpoint Administrator Associate (MD-102) certification.
COMPENSATION:

Dexian reasonably expects to offer $38.00 - $45.00 per hour for this position based on location, skills and experience. Additional benefits include generous time off, health, vision and dental insurance and matching 401k.
ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:

Submit/Pass a 10-year background check, criminal history, drug screening, and fingerprints.Valid driver's license/Real-ID with clean driver's history.Applicants must have a strong work ethic, be extremely organized and detail-oriented, be a self-starter with excellent time management, problem solving, and multitasking skills.Excellent face-to-face customer service skills - at least 1 year experience in a customer service position, such as: call center, retail, or other customer facing role.Demonstrated problem solving skills with ability to research, resolve and complete tasks within pre-determined deadlines and schedules.Able to process work quickly and accurately despite interruptions, changing timelines and priorities, on a regular basis.Strong command of Microsoft Office Suite including Outlook, Excel, and Word programs.Working knowledge of app/database ticketing systems. Knowledge of ServiceNow, a plus.Position may require dexterity and ability to sit, stand, walk extended distances.

Dexian stands at the forefront of Talent + Technology solutions with a presence spanning more than 70 locations worldwide and a team exceeding 10,000 professionals. As one of the largest technology and professional staffing companies and one of the largest minority-owned staffing companies in the United States, Dexian combines over 30 years of industry expertise with cutting-edge technologies to deliver comprehensive global services and support.

Dexian connects the right talent and the right technology with the right organizations to deliver trajectory-changing results that help everyone achieve their ambitions and goals. To learn more, please visit https://dexian.com/.

Dexian is an Equal Opportunity Employer that recruits and hires qualified candidates without regard to race, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, disability, or veteran status.

jobs.summary: Leidos Dig Mod Health IT division is seeking a skilled and collaborative Platform System Administrator and Engineer to support a federal agency’s extensive macOS and Windows infrastructure. This role requires a deep technical acumen combined with strong...
jobs.description:
Leidos Dig Mod Health IT division is seeking a skilled and collaborative Platform System Administrator and Engineer to support a federal agency’s extensive macOS and Windows infrastructure. This role requires a deep technical acumen combined with strong interpersonal skills, as the candidate will be responsible for managing and optimizing Apple macOS devices through JAMF Pro and Microsoft Windows devices via Microsoft Intune and Azure. The ideal candidate will excel in both technical problem-solving and effective communication, working seamlessly with cross-functional teams to ensure top-tier support and operational excellence.

Candidate MUST:

Be a US Citizen or US Person who has lived in the United States for at least three consecutive years and an active Public Trust clearance is preferred.

Six (6) additional years of experience may be substituted in lieu of a Bachelors Degree.Minimum of 5–7 years of hands-on experience in system administration and endpoint/device management.Extensive experience managing macOS devices via Jamf Pro in complex enterprise environments.Substantial experience supporting Windows devices in mixed-platform environments including proven expertise in Mobile Device Management (MDM) using Microsoft Intune, and Azure.Create and manage configuration profiles, policies, smart groups, scripts, and packages.Develop automated workflows for provisioning, patching, compliance enforcement, and software distribution.Experience with scripting languages (Bash, Python, PowerShell)Knowledge of MDM protocols and Apple Business Manager/Apple School ManagerExpertise in endpoint management and system administration.
Preferred Qualifications:

Public Trust level 4 clearance or similar JAMF 300, JAMF 400, Apple Certified MacOS Technician (ACMT), etc.Familiarity with integration between JAMF and other enterprise systems (Azure AD, SSO solutions)Knowledge of cloud services (iCloud, Google Workspace, Microsoft 365)Integrate macOS management solutions with federal security tools such as identity providers, endpoint protection, SIEM, vulnerability scanners, or compliance platforms.In-depth understanding of endpoint security best practices, including system hardening and compliance.Work closely with federal stakeholders, system administrators, network engineers, cybersecurity teams, and program leadership.Participate in change management processes, technical reviews, and configuration control boards.Provide tier 3 technical escalation support and guidance for IT staff.Experience in overseeing or supporting IT projects, including upgrades, migrations, and deployments. Ability to plan, execute, and manage project tasks effectively.Excellent written and verbal communication and collaboration skills to work effectively with diverse teams, contractors, and stakeholders. Ability to build and maintain strong working relationships and deliver exceptional customer service.Advanced analytical skills to address complex issues, develop strategic solutions, and execute them effectively. Strong troubleshooting capabilities and a proactive approach to problem resolution.Expertise in creating and maintaining detailed technical documentation and process improvements. Ability to design and implement standard operating procedures.

jobs.title: Operations Systems Engineer
jobs.summary: Operations Systems Engineer The Opportunity: Are you looking for an opportunity to combine your technical skills with big picture thinking to make an impact in the DoD? You understand your customer's environment and how to develop the right systems for...
jobs.description:
Operations Systems Engineer The Opportunity: Are you looking for an opportunity to combine your technical

skills with big picture thinking to make an impact in the DoD? You understand your customer's environment and how to develop the right systems for their mission. Your ability to translate real world needs into technical specifications makes you an integral part of delivering a customer focused engineering solution. As an engineer on our team, you'll work with Booz Allen colleagues and the U.S. Space Force to develop and rapidly deploy the next generation of resilient Missile Warning (MW), Missile Tracking (MT), and Tactical Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (TISR) capabilities to deter attacks and provide critical information to our warfighters to deter and defeat our enemies in battle. jobs.title: Windows and MacOS Endpoint Engineer II
jobs.summary: We are seeking a highly skilled Windows and macOS Endpoint Engineer II to join our IT Infrastructure team. This role is responsible for designing, implementing, and managing enterprise endpoint management solutions across Windows and macOS environments....
jobs.description:
We are seeking a highly skilled Windows and macOS Endpoint Engineer II to join our IT Infrastructure team. This role is responsible for designing, implementing, and managing enterprise endpoint management solutions across Windows and macOS environments. The ideal candidate will have extensive experience with Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM), Intune, Jamf Pro, Azure, Active Directory, and endpoint automation while helping build a modern, secure, and scalable desktop platform for end users.

Job Title: Windows and macOS Endpoint Engineer II
Location: Newark, DE (Onsite)
Contract Duration: 8+ Months
Rate Range: $65–80/hr on W2

Responsibilities:

Design, implement, and maintain enterprise endpoint management solutions for Windows and macOS devices.

Manage application deployment, configuration management, asset management, and endpoint lifecycle using MECM, Intune, and Jamf Pro.

Automate endpoint management, user provisioning, and data migration using PowerShell, Bash, Python, or similar scripting languages.

Design, implement, and maintain Azure Conditional Access, compliance policies, and endpoint security configurations.

Configure and manage Active Directory and Azure Group Policies.

Design, test, and implement Virtual Desktop solutions.

Develop and maintain endpoint security standards, including patch management, encryption, antivirus, and least-privilege access controls.

Monitor endpoint health, compliance, and performance while developing dashboards and KPIs.

Research, design, and implement secure, scalable, and highly available endpoint solutions.

Create implementation plans, test plans, project timelines, and technical documentation.

Mentor junior engineers and provide operational guidance across endpoint management platforms.

Collaborate with IT teams, project teams, vendors, and business stakeholders to deliver enterprise endpoint services.

Drive continuous improvement through automation, governance, and operational best practices.

Required Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field, or 5+ years of equivalent endpoint engineering experience.

Strong experience managing enterprise Windows and macOS environments.

Hands-on expertise with Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM), Microsoft Intune, and Jamf Pro.

Experience implementing Azure Conditional Access, compliance policies, and endpoint security controls.

Experience with Active Directory and Azure Group Policy administration.

Experience designing and implementing Virtual Desktop solutions.

Strong scripting experience for endpoint automation and user data migration.

Knowledge of endpoint security best practices, including patch management, encryption, antivirus, and least-privilege security models.

Excellent troubleshooting, analytical, and communication skills.

Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced enterprise environment.

Required Qualifications:

Endpoint Management

Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM)

Microsoft Intune

Jamf Pro

Microsoft Autopilot

Endpoint Lifecycle Management

Application Deployment

Asset Management

Microsoft Technologies

Azure Active Directory

Active Directory

Azure Conditional Access

Group Policy (GPO)

Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI)

Automation & Scripting

PowerShell

Bash

Python

Endpoint Automation

User Data Migration

Security & Compliance

Endpoint Security

Patch Management

Device Compliance

Encryption

Antivirus

Least Privilege Security Model

Infrastructure & Governance

Endpoint Governance

Change Management

Configuration Management

Dashboard Development

KPI Reporting

Process Documentation

Preferred Qualifications:

Experience implementing enterprise endpoint management solutions across large, geographically distributed organizations.

Experience designing deployment workflows using Jamf Pro and Microsoft Autopilot.

Microsoft Certified: Modern Desktop Administrator Associate certification.

Jamf Certified Tech or related certifications.

Experience building enterprise endpoint automation frameworks.

Strong knowledge of endpoint architecture, governance, and operational best practices.

If you meet the required qualifications and are interested in this role, please apply today.

The Solomon Page Distinction

Solomon Page offers a comprehensive benefit program for hourly employees. We pride ourselves on offering medical, dental, 401(k), direct deposit and commuter benefits to our employees, including freelancers - which sets us apart in the industries we serve.

About Solomon Page

Founded in 1990, Solomon Page is a specialty niche provider of staffing and executive search solutions across a wide array of functions and industries. The success of Solomon Page reflects an organic growth strategy supported by a highly entrepreneurial culture. Acting as a strategic partner to our clients and candidates, we focus on providing customized solutions and building long-term relationships based on trust, respect, and the consistent delivery of excellent results. For more information and additional opportunities, visit: solomonpage.com and connect with us on Facebook, and LinkedIn.

jobs.title: End User Computing Engineer (Onsite)
jobs.summary: Overview: We have an exciting End User Computing Engineer Opportunity for an experienced professional to join our team at Pediatrix Medical Group. We are a national organization, and one of the nation’s largest providers of prenatal, neonatal and...
jobs.description:
Overview:

We have an exciting End User Computing Engineer Opportunity for an experienced professional to join our team at Pediatrix Medical Group. We are a national organization, and one of the nation’s largest providers of prenatal, neonatal and pediatric services.

Talented business professionals from diverse backgrounds choose Pediatrix because we are an exciting and innovative company that focuses on a team approach to improve the lives of patients everywhere. We are confident that you'll love being a part of the Pediatrix team.
Responsibilities:

The End User Computing (EUC) Engineer is responsible for the overall lifecycle (plan, build, run) of all enterprise endpoints including laptops, desktops and mobile devices. Develops and maintains system images, application packages and patch management for all endpoints in the enterprise. Partners with other key engineering teams and security to ensure strategy and architecture support the overall IT strategy and key system integrations. You will be a recognized Subject Matter Expert and manage large projects and processes with suitable support. You will be willing to work with colleagues to improve the skills of others in the team. Your outputs are likely to have a broad impact on the business with high visibility.

Design, develop & maintain all system images for laptops, desktops & mobile devices.
Certify all new hardware and software for endpoints in the environment prior to purchase or release.
Leverage modern industry leading deployment technologies for overall endpoint management that also optimizes the employee experience
Updates and patches all OS & application patches and vulnerabilities
Creates and maintains application packages developing a software store for employees to download required software
Ensures all endpoints meet security requirements
Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune) administration
Design and maintain Microsoft Azure AD management as it impacts the endpoint space
Audits and analyzes data to ensure processes are followed and standards are maintained
Provides tier 3 desktop support
Works independently on larger, moderately complex projects/ assignments.
Responsible for making adjustments or recommended enhancements to systems and processes to solve problems or improve effectiveness of job area.
Problems and issues faced are general and may require understanding of broader set of issues but typically are not complex.
Works on problems that may require understanding of other job areas.
May provide guidance and assistance to entry level professionals and/or support employees.
Qualifications:

Education/Experience:

2 years’ experience in the end user compute space
Experience in endpoint management: install, support, configure, evaluate, maintain, monitor, and analyze systems and software in both mobile and PC devices.
Experience with application upgrades, security patching, and platform updates
Strong experience with Microsoft Autopilot, Intune/endpoint manager

Knowledge/Skills:

Understanding of hardware and software dependencies
Ability to translate business requirements to technical requirements
Strong team player who can work across multiple functional areas and disciplines with attention to detail
Benefits and Compensation:

Take great care of the patient, every day and every way.TM At Pediatrix & Obstetrix, that’s not only our motto at work each day; it’s also how we view our employees and their families. We know that our greatest asset is YOU.

We take pride in offering comprehensive benefits in a vast array of plans that fit your life and lifestyle, supporting your health and overall well-being. Benefits offered include, but are not limited to: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life, Disability, Healthcare FSA, Dependent Care FSA and HSAs, as well as a 401k plan and Employee Stock Purchase Program. Some benefits are provided at no cost, while others require a cost share between employees and the company. Employees may also select voluntary plans and pay for these benefits through convenient payroll deductions. Our benefit programs are just one of the many ways Pediatrix & Obstetrix helps our employees take care of themselves and their families.
About Us:

Pediatrix Medical Group is one of the nation’s leading providers of highly specialized health care for women, babies and children. Since 1979, Pediatrix has grown from a single neonatology practice to a national, multi-specialty medical group. Pediatrix-affiliated clinicians are committed to providing coordinated, compassionate and clinically excellent services to women, babies and children across the continuum of care, both in hospital settings and office-based practices. The group’s high-quality, evidence-based care is bolstered by significant investments in research, education, quality-improvement and safety initiatives.

Please Note: Fraudulent job postings/job scams are becoming increasingly common. All genuine Pediatrix job postings can be found through the Pediatrix Careers site: www.pediatrix.com/careers.

#LI-Remote

#PedCorp
Pediatrix is an Equal Opportunity Employer:

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.

Job Description:

DESKTOP APPLICATION PACKAGING & DEPLOYMENT SYSTEMS ADMINISTRATOR

YOUR IMPACT

Own your opportunity to support national defense. Your work will help keep critical operations secure and connected across the global enterprise directly, contributing to a mission that protects our nation every day.

CIO Infrastructure Services (CIS) is growing, and we invite both our experienced incumbents and new team members to be part of what's next. Incumbents bring invaluable mission insight, while new talent adds fresh ideas and energy. Together, we'll strengthen continuity and drive the next generation of innovation.

MEANINGFUL WORK AND PERSONAL IMPACT

As a Desktop Application Packaging & Deployment Systems Administrator supporting CIS, you will ensure stability, security, and performance of enterprise desktop applications across a globally distributed environment. You will administer and support core user-facing software services, maintain application configurations, and deliver reliable, secure capabilities that enable users to operate efficiently. Your work directly strengthens productivity, standardization, and application readiness across mission critical systems.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Package, test, and deploy enterprise desktop applications using approved deployment technologies such as MECM, Intune, and automated task sequencesStandardize desktop operating system builds, ensuring configurations, baselines, drivers, and imaging processes remain current and compliantManage application versioning, update cycles, EOL planning, and controlled rollout of new or refreshed application packages across multiple networksTroubleshoot application deployment, OS build, and packaging issues involving dependencies, profiles, permissions, and network related elementsSupport virtualized and physical client environments, including thick, thin, and VDI based clients, ensuring consistent delivery of approved applicationsMaintain packaging documentation, SOPs, and submission packages for approval, accreditation, and operational useCollaborate with engineering, cybersecurity, endpoint management, and operations teams to validate requirements and maintain secure, compliant deploymentsIntegrate approved drivers, firmware, and hardware related software components into automated deployment workflowsImplement and validate application, OS, and client-side security patches in accordance with vulnerability management and change control timelinesMonitor systems for patch and configuration compliance; identify noncompliant endpoints and support remediation and POA&M documentation

WHAT YOU'LL NEED TO SUCCEED:

EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:
Systems Administrator (Senior)-Desktop Application Packaging - Requires a Bachelor's degree in an ITrelated field or 8+ years of relevant IT experience in lieu of a degree.
Systems Administrator (Principal)-Desktop Application Packaging - Requires a Bachelor's degree in an ITrelated field or 10+ years of relevant IT experience in lieu of a degree.

CERTIFICATION: Meet DoD 8140 Information Assurance Workforce certification requirements (e.g. Security+ CE, etc.)CLEARANCE: Active TS/SCI clearance with a favorable polygraphHands-on experience administering enterprise desktop applications, client services, or endpoint management platformsStrong understanding of application deployment, configuration baselines, software lifecycle, and secure implementation practicesMaintain proficiency with required toolsets, including Windows Desktop OS, Microsoft Deployment Toolkit (MDT), Active Directory and Group Policy, PowerShell and Batch scripting, AppV, and Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager to support enterprise application packaging and deploymentFamiliarity with patching processes, vulnerability management, and compliance requirements across enterprise desktop environmentsAbility to troubleshoot complex application issues across distributed systemsStrong documentation, communication, and cross team collaboration skillsAbility to support CONUS/OCONUS travel as requiredUS citizenship required

LOCATION: Onsite

GDIT IS YOUR PLACE
At GDIT, the mission is our purpose, and our people are at the center of everything we do.
Growth: AI-powered career tool that identifies career steps and learning opportunities
Support: An internal mobility team focused on helping you achieve your career goals
Rewards: Comprehensive benefits and wellness packages, 401K with company match, and competitive pay and paid time off
Community: Award-winning culture of innovation and a military-friendly workplace

OWN YOUR OPPORTUNITY
Explore an enterprise IT career at GDIT and you'll find endless opportunities to grow alongside colleagues who share your desire to drive operations forward.

#CIS

The likely salary range for this position is $72,877 - $98,599. This is not, however, a guarantee of compensation or salary. Rather, salary will be set based on experience, geographic location and possibly contractual requirements and could fall outside of this range.

40

10-25%

USA CO Colorado Springs - 8610 Explorer Dr (COC036), USA DC Washington, USA FL MacDill AFB - 7115 S Boundary Blvd (FLC096), USA VA Springfield - 7770 Backlick Rd (VAS110)

Total Rewards at GDIT:

Our benefits package for all US-based employees includes a variety of medical plan options, some with Health Savings Accounts, dental plan options, a vision plan, and a 401(k) plan offering the ability to contribute both pre and post-tax dollars up to the IRS annual limits and receive a company match. To encourage work/life balance, GDIT offers employees full flex work weeks where possible and a variety of paid time off plans, including vacation, sick and personal time, holidays, paid parental, military, bereavement and jury duty leave. To ensure our employees are able to protect their income, other offerings such as short and long-term disability benefits, life, accidental death and dismemberment, personal accident, critical illness and business travel and accident insurance are provided or available. We regularly review our Total Rewards package to ensure our offerings are competitive and reflect what our employees have told us they value most.

Our Identity Verification Process:

As part of the hiring process, we will ask you to complete an identity verification process that leverages advanced biometrics and artificial intelligence to ensure authenticity and protect against identity fraud. You are expected to be on camera during virtual interviews. We reserve the right to take your picture to verify your identity and prevent fraud. By proceeding, you authorize the collection, processing, and use of your biometric data for identity verification and security purposes.

About Our Work:

We are GDIT. A global technology and professional services company that delivers technology solutions and mission services to every major agency across the U.S. government, defense and intelligence community. Our 26,000 experts extract the power of technology to create immediate value and deliver solutions at the edge of innovation. We operate across 50+ countries worldwide, offering leading mission-ready capabilities in AI, cloud, cyber and software development.

Join our Talent Community to stay up to date on our career opportunities and events atgdit.com/tc.

jobs.title: Senior Endpoint Management Engineer
jobs.summary: We are looking for a Senior Endpoint Management Engineer to own, maintain, and evolve the platforms that manage our entire device fleet. You will be the technical authority for two complementary MDM ecosystems — Microsoft Intune (Windows laptops, iPhones,...
jobs.description:
We are looking for a Senior Endpoint Management Engineer to own, maintain, and evolve the platforms that manage our entire device fleet. You will be the technical authority for two complementary MDM ecosystems — Microsoft Intune (Windows laptops, iPhones, and iPads) and Kandji / IRU (Apple MacBooks) — making sure every device is secure, compliant, and effortless to use from day one.

This is a hands‑on engineering role for someone who treats endpoint management as a product: automated, measurable, and built around the end‑user experience. As a financial‑services technology (fintech) company operating in a highly regulated industry, we adopt cutting‑edge technology to support rapid business growth without compromising on security. You will sit at the center of that mission — helping shift IT from a reactive, manual support model toward a proactive, automation‑driven platform.

Key Responsibilities
Endpoint & MDM platform ownership

Administer, maintain, and continuously improve Microsoft Intune (Windows laptops, iPhones, iPads) and Kandji / IRU (Apple MacBooks).

Own the full device lifecycle: zero‑touch enrollment via Apple Business Manager (ADE) and Windows Autopilot, configuration, app deployment, patching, and retirement.

Define and enforce configuration profiles, compliance policies, and baseline standards across all platforms and OS versions.

Maintain integrations across the wider stack: Microsoft 365, Microsoft Azure / Entra ID, Microsoft Defender, Cisco Meraki, and Zscaler.

Automation & AI

Identify repetitive, manual, and error‑prone tasks and replace them with automation (e.g., Intune Proactive Remediations, scripting, Microsoft Graph API, Kandji automation).

Champion and implement AI‑driven automations — self‑service, self‑healing, automated remediation, and assisted support — to improve the end‑user experience and reduce service desk workload.

Deliver measurable impact: fewer tickets, faster resolution, and less manual intervention.

Security & compliance (fintech‑grade)

Implement and maintain Zero Trust controls: Conditional Access, device compliance gating, encryption (BitLocker / FileVault), and least‑privilege access.

Manage endpoint threat protection through Microsoft Defender and ensure secure connectivity via Zscaler.

Align endpoint configuration and evidence with regulatory and audit requirements — ISO 27001, SOC 2, DORA, and GDPR — and support internal and external audits.

End‑user experience

Deliver fast, reliable, zero‑touch onboarding so new joiners are productive on day one.

Proactively monitor device health and performance; resolve issues before users notice them.

Act as the senior escalation point for complex endpoint issues raised by the service desk.

Collaboration & continuous improvement

Partner with IT Support, IT Operations, Security, and Infrastructure teams, keeping ownership boundaries and escalation paths clear.

Document standards, runbooks, and knowledge‑base articles to enable the wider team.

Track and report on endpoint KPIs (compliance rate, patch coverage, enrollment success, ticket deflection) and drive continual improvement.

Must Have

3–5 years of hands‑on experience administering MDM / endpoint management platforms in an enterprise environment.

Proven expertise with Microsoft Intune across Windows and iOS / iPadOS, and with macOS management via Kandji (or a comparable Apple MDM such as Jamf).

Strong working knowledge of Apple Business Manager, Automated Device Enrollment (ADE), and Windows Autopilot.

Solid grounding in Microsoft 365 and Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD), including Conditional Access and compliance policies.

Scripting and automation skills (PowerShell, Bash, and/or Microsoft Graph API).

Practical understanding of endpoint security and compliance in a regulated environment.

Preferred / Nice to Have

Relevant certifications: Microsoft 365 Certified: Endpoint Administrator Associate (MD-102), Apple, or Kandji certifications.

Hands‑on experience with Microsoft Defender, Cisco Meraki, and Zscaler.

Experience applying AI tooling (e.g., Copilot, AI‑assisted scripting or support agents) to IT operations.

Prior experience in fintech, financial services, or another regulated industry (ISO 27001 / SOC 2 / DORA / GDPR).

Who You Are

Proactive —you anticipate problems and fix root causes instead of reacting to tickets.

A team player —you collaborate openly, share knowledge, and make the whole team better.

Forward‑looking —genuinely excited about automation and AI as tools to make IT faster and simpler.

Security‑minded —secure by default, with the judgment to balance protection and usability.

A clear communicator —comfortable with technical peers and non‑technical end users alike.

jobs.description:
Job Title: Senior Intune Administrator / Specialist
Location: Stamford, CT 06901 (Fully Onsite)
Duration: 12-months CTH

Role Overview:
We are seeking a Senior Intune Administrator to lead the architecture, configuration, and deployment of Microsoft Intune across our enterprise environment. The ideal candidate is not just a support technician, but a high-level specialist capable of driving our modern endpoint management strategy from the ground up.
Your primary mission will be to establish Intune as our primary security platform and patching methodology, migrate legacy Group Policies to modern equivalents, and ensure a seamless deployment. Once properly configured and documented, this environment will be handed off to junior staff for day-to-day operations.
Technical Qualifications & Experience: Expert-Level Intune Experience: Proven track record of successfully architecting and deploying Microsoft Intune in an enterprise environment (this is not a "support-only" role).
Endpoint Security Specialist: Deep understanding of using Intune as a security platform, including Conditional Access, Compliance Policies, and Windows Defender/Endpoint Security integration.
Modern Management & GPOs: Strong experience migrating legacy on-premise infrastructure (GPOs) to cloud-native MDM equivalents.
Patching Infrastructure: Expertise in designing and managing Windows Update for Business (WUfB) and patch deployment strategies within Intune.
Active Directory Integration: Solid experience with Azure AD / Entra ID, hybrid identity setups, and device enrollment methods (Autopilot, co-management).
Scripting & Automation: Strong PowerShell scripting skills for custom configurations, packaging, and automated remediation tasks.

jobs.summary: Overview Since our founding in 1924, we've cut cardiovascular disease deaths in half, but there is still so much more to do. To overcome today’s biggest health challenges and accelerate this progress, we need passionate individuals like you. Join our...
jobs.description:
Overview

Since our founding in 1924, we've cut cardiovascular disease deaths in half, but there is still so much more to do. To overcome today’s biggest health challenges and accelerate this progress, we need passionate individuals like you. Join our movement, be part of the progress, and help ensure a healthier future for all. You matter, and so does the impact you can make with us.

We are seeking a skilled Endpoint Management System Administrator to support and operate a modern enterprise endpoint environment. This is a highly hands-on operational role responsible for administering and maintaining enterprise endpoint management platforms, with daily use of Microsoft Intune and Tanium to manage endpoint configuration, compliance, patching, and software deployments.

The ideal candidate will have strong experience managing Windows and macOS endpoints, with working knowledge of Linux, and will be comfortable performing daily endpoint administration tasks using Microsoft Intune and Tanium. These platforms are used extensively for endpoint visibility, machine compliance, vulnerability remediation, software updates, and software deployments. Jamf is also used to manage macOS systems within the environment.

The Association offers many resources to help you maintain work-life harmonization through your changing needs and life situations. To help you be successful, you will have access to Heart U, our award-winning corporate university, as well as additional training and support, locally.

#TheAHALife is more than a company culture; it is our way of life. It embodies our commitment to work-life harmonization and is guided by our core values where our employees can thrive both personally and professionally. Discover why you will Be Seen. Be Heard. Be Valued at the American Heart Association by following us on LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, X, and at heart.jobs.

Responsibilities

Administer and support enterprise endpoints across Windows desktops and servers, macOS, and Linux.

Use Microsoft Intune and Tanium as primary endpoint management platforms for daily endpoint administration and operational management such as:

Endpoint visibility and real-time inventory

Machine compliance monitoring and enforcement

Operating system and third-party software updates

Software packaging, deployments, and remediation

Vulnerability identification and remediation

Manage Windows endpoints using Microsoft Intune and Group Policy Objects (GPO).

Manage macOS endpoints using Jamf, including configuration profiles, policies, and application deployments.

Execute and track vulnerability remediation efforts, ensuring timely patching and risk reduction across all endpoint platforms.

Support and maintain enterprise cloud print solutions.

Collaborate with cybersecurity, infrastructure, and service teams to ensure endpoint configurations align with security standards and operational requirements.

Implementation and management of endpoint security baselines and configuration standards aligned with CIS Security Controls.

Create and maintain documentation, standard operating procedures, and operational runbooks.

Participate in continuous improvement initiatives focused on strengthening endpoint security, automation, and operational efficiency.

Qualifications

Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience

Minimum of Three (3) years of relevant experience

Proven experience managing enterprise desktops and servers in a large-scale environment.

Hands-on operational experience administering endpoints using Microsoft Intune.Strong working knowledge of:

Microsoft Intune (Windows endpoint management)

Windows Autopilot

Group Policy (GPO)

Jamf (macOS management)

Experience administering Windows desktop and server environments.

Working knowledge of macOS and Linux administration.

Demonstrated experience managing machine compliance, software patching, and vulnerability remediation.

Strong troubleshooting skills and experience operating in a large, complex enterprise environment.

Experience implementing or supporting device compliance policies and their integration with conditional access controls using Microsoft Intune and Microsoft Entra ID.

Preferred Qualifications

Additional hands-on experience using Tanium for endpoint management, compliance reporting, patching, and software deployment.

Experience managing third-party application updates across diverse endpoint platforms.

Familiarity with modern endpoint security concepts such as least privilege, zero trust, and compliance baselines.

Experience supporting cloud-first or hybrid endpoint management strategies.

#AHAINDP

Compensation & Benefits

The expected pay range will be $95,000 - $105,000. Pay is commensurate with experience; geographic differentials to the pay range may apply. The American Heart Association reserves the right to pay more or less than the posted range.

The American Heart Association invests in its people. Here are the main components of our total rewards package. Visit Rewards & Benefits to see more details.

Compensation – Our goal is to ensure you have a competitive base salary. That’s why we regularly review the market value of jobs and make adjustments, as needed.

Performance and Recognition – You are rewarded for achieving success through annual salary planning and incentive programs; based on the type of position

Benefits – We offer a wide array of benefits including medical, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance, along with a robust retirement program that includes an employer match and automatic contribution. As a mark of our commitment to employee well-being, we also offer an employee assistance program, employee wellness program and telemedicine, and medical consultation.

Professional Development – You can join one of our many Employee Resource Groups (ERG) or be a mentor/mentee in our professional mentoring program. Heart U is the Association’s national online university, with more than 100,000 resources designed to meet your needs and busy schedule.

Work-Life Harmonization – The Association offers Paid Time Off (PTO) at a minimum of 16 days per year for new employees. The number of days will increase based on seniority level. You will also have a total of 12 paid holidays off each year, which includes several days off at the end of the year.

Tuition Assistance - We support the career development of all employees. This program provides financial assistance to employees who wish to further their education and career in relation to their current duties and responsibilities, or for potential future positions in the organization.

The American Heart Association’s 2028 Goal: Building on over 100 years of trusted leadership in cardiovascular and brain health, by 2028 the Association will drive breakthroughs and implement proven solutions in science, policy, and care for healthier people and communities. The greatest discoveries in health must reach everyone where they are.

At American Heart Association | American Stroke Association, our mission is to be a relentless force for a world of longer, healthier lives, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, religion, age, language, sexual orientation, national origin and physical or cognitive abilities.

jobs.description:
Position: SCCM Engineer

Contract to Hire

Location: Hybrid, 4x (M-Th) Fort Lauderdale, FL

Role Overview:

Overview

We are seeking a Senior SCCM / MECM Engineer to support and enhance our enterprise endpoint management environment. This individual will be responsible for maintaining and optimizing Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM/SCCM), operating system deployment, software packaging, patch management, and endpoint lifecycle management across a large-scale Windows environment.

The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in endpoint management technologies, application deployment, Windows server administration, PowerShell automation, and enterprise systems support. This is a highly hands-on engineering role requiring both operational support and continuous improvement of the endpoint management ecosystem.

Key Responsibilities

Administer and maintain Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM/SCCM) infrastructure.
Design, implement, and optimize software deployment and application packaging solutions.
Manage Windows workstation and server patching through MECM/SCCM and WSUS.
Create and maintain operating system deployment (OSD) task sequences.
Troubleshoot endpoint management, deployment, and patching issues.
Support Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, and related Windows infrastructure services.
Develop and maintain PowerShell scripts to automate system administration and deployment activities.
Monitor system health, performance, and compliance metrics.
Partner with infrastructure, security, and application teams to support enterprise initiatives.
Perform root cause analysis and implement long-term solutions for recurring issues.
Document processes, standards, and technical configurations.
Provide support for major upgrades, migrations, and enterprise technology projects.

Required Qualifications

5+ years of hands-on experience administering MECM/SCCM in an enterprise environment.
Strong experience with:

Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM/SCCM)
WSUS
Windows 10/11 endpoint management
Windows Server administration
Active Directory
Group Policy
DNS

Experience with Operating System Deployment (OSD) and task sequence management.
Experience with software packaging and application deployment.
Strong PowerShell scripting and automation skills.
Experience troubleshooting endpoint deployment and patch management issues.
Excellent communication and customer support skills.

Preferred Qualifications

Microsoft Intune and co-management experience.
Knowledge of Microsoft Endpoint Manager ecosystem.
Experience supporting hybrid on-premises and cloud environments.
Familiarity with vulnerability remediation and endpoint security practices.
Experience working in large enterprise environments with thousands of endpoints.
ITIL or Microsoft certifications are a plus.

Technical Environment

MECM / SCCM
WSUS
Microsoft Intune
Active Directory
Group Policy
PowerShell
Windows Server
Windows 10/11
SQL Server
Enterprise Endpoint Management

Ideal Background

Candidates should have experience managing enterprise-scale endpoint environments, including software deployment, patch management, operating system deployments, infrastructure support, automation, and ongoing optimization of the Windows endpoint ecosystem.
