jobs.description:
AMO is looking for a Windows Systems Engineer to work in North Charleston, SC. The MECM/SCCM Windows Engineer provides advanced endpoint engineering and enterprise Windows infrastructure support within the NIWC Atlantic RDT&E environment. This role is responsible for Windows image engineering, endpoint configuration management, software deployment, system integration, and sustainment across classified and unclassified networks using System Center Configuration Manager as well as Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager. To join our team of outstanding professionals, apply today!

Responsibilities

The engineer will support multi-tenant lab environments and enterprise services while ensuring compliance with DoD cybersecurity requirements and operational continuity.Manage and deploy Windows baseline images using SCCM/MECM.Create and deploy task sequences in SCCM/MECM, including Windows migrations, custom image deployments, and configuration management.Deploy third-party software using standard SCCM/MECM best practices or through third-party repositories integrated with SCCM/MECM.Integrate enterprise tools into SCCM/MECM environments, including WSUS, BitLocker, Windows Deployment Services (WDS), ManageEngine, Dell Command, and other related tools and services.Perform installation, configuration, maintenance, and security hardening within classified and unclassified network environments.Conduct technical planning, system hardware/software/network design, development, integration, verification and validation (V&V), and fielding activities.Provide technical support, administration, and monitoring of Microsoft Windows systems within physical and virtual environments.Support operations, maintenance, and troubleshooting of end-user workstations and desktop applications.Lead data collection, conduct analysis, and develop and implement solutions associated with incident tickets and operational requirements.Identify and implement opportunities for continuous improvement to enhance operational effectiveness and efficiency.Develop and document solutions to complex technical issues.Maintain all qualification(s) required under the Cybersecurity Workforce (CSWF) framework for Privileged Access Administrator (PAA) roles.
Qualifications

Active Top-Secret Clearance.7+ years of experience supporting enterprise infrastructure in a DoD environment.Experience administering and engineering Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM/SCCM).Experience supporting Windows enterprise environments in classified and unclassified networks.Ability to perform technical planning, system integration, and validation activities.DoD 8570/8140 compliant certification (e.g., Security+) Desired Certifications / Training:

Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM) Training (23 hours).Windows Server 2019 or 2022 administration training.Windows 11 administration training.
Job ID

2026-23367
Work Type

On-Site
Company Description

Work Where it Matters

Akima Mission Optimization (AMO), an Akima company, is not just another federal logistics contractor. As an Alaska Native Corporation (ANC), our mission and purpose extend beyond our exciting federal projects as we support our shareholder communities in Alaska.

At AMO, the work you do every day makes a difference in the lives of our 15,000 Iñupiat shareholders, a group of Alaska natives from one of the most remote and harshest environments in the United States.

For our shareholders, AMO provides support and employment opportunities and contributes to the survival of a culture that has thrived above the Arctic Circle for more than 10,000 years.

For our government customers, AMO delivers innovative administrative support services that streamline operations, and enhance productivity.

As an AMO employee, you will be surrounded by a challenging, yet supportive work environment that is committed to innovation and diversity, two of our most important values. You will also have access to our comprehensive benefits and competitive pay in addition to growth opportunities and excellent retirement options.

jobs.title: Senior Systems Engineer (Windows Endpoint Mangement)
jobs.summary: About Gartner IT: Join a world-class team of skilled engineers who build creative digital solutions to support our colleagues and clients. We make a broad organizational impact by delivering cutting-edge technology solutions that power Gartner. Gartner IT...
jobs.description:
About Gartner IT:

Join a world-class team of skilled engineers who build creative digital solutions to support our colleagues and clients. We make a broad organizational impact by delivering cutting-edge technology solutions that power Gartner. Gartner IT values its culture of nonstop innovation, an outcome-driven approach to success, and the notion that great ideas can come from anyone on the team.

About the role:

The Windows Endpoint team is looking for an engineer to help manage the Windows fleet including endpoint management, patching, administration and automation using technologies such as Intune, Configuration Manager, and PowerShell.

What you will do

Design, implement and maintain our Windows-based endpoint environment, including endpoint management, patch management, security management and deployment automation

Evaluate new technologies related to end user computing for use in the corporate enterprise to enable our knowledge workers

Develop standard work environments on company owned devices to ensure usability, performance and security requirements are maintained

Automate end user support activities to include packaging of software and scripting of support tasks

Automate service desk and desktop support tasks using various scripting languages (PowerShell)

Assist with packaging all Gartner owned software for deployment via Enterprise distribution methods using Configuration Manager (formerly System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM)) and Microsoft Intune

Leverage DEX (Digital Employee Experience) tools to proactively optimize and manage the employee experience on Windows endpoints.

Lead the design and development of DEX-based remote actions and dashboards to drive actionable insights and improve operational efficiency.

Administration of Configuration Manager and Intune to include metrics/reporting using various tools

Leverage AI-driven tools (e.g., Gemini, Copilot) to assist in scripting, diagnostics, hypothesis generation, and resolution drafting.

Stay up-to-date with industry trends and emerging endpoint security and management technologies

Work with the team to coach Gartner's internal support teams via email communications, videos, virtual training sessions, and the creation/maintenance of knowledgebase articles and support documentation.

Work within ITIL Change Management process to shepherd planned changes

What you will need :

2+ years managing the Automation of tasks using various scripting languages (PowerShell) to better support the service desk and desktop support

3+ years of experience using Intune/Configuration Manager to manage and deploy Windows devices

Packaging and distribution of applications using Configuration Manager.

Experience with Windows Patching using Windows Autopatch/Windows Update for Business, WSUS/CM, Software Update Groups (SUG), and Automatic Deployment Rules (ADR), 3rd party patching.

Good to have:

Hybrid Active Directory, Azure Active Directory

Microsoft Azure

2+ years of experience managing devices using Configuration Manager (aka CM, SCCM, MECM)

Experience using Autopilot to deploy Windows devices via Microsoft Intune

Supporting Windows 365 Cloud PCs, Azure Virtual Desktop (Windows Virtual Desktop) or another similar VDI solution (Citrix, Amazon Workspaces)

Active Directory Users and Computers

PowerBI and reporting

Intune Configuration policies/Group Policy

Experience with DEX (Digital Employee Experience) tools

Experience with Microsoft Windows

Experience with Microsoft Office 365 and Office Configuration Manager

Who you are:

Graduate/Post graduate in BE/Btech, ME/MTech or MCA is preferred.

Excellent communication and prioritization skills.

Able to work independently or within a team proactively in a fast-paced AGILE-SCRUM environment.

Owns success – Takes responsibility for successful delivery of the solutions.

Strong desire to improve upon their skills in software development, frameworks, and technologies

What you will get:

Competitive compensation.

Limitless growth and learning opportunities.

Ongoing mentorship and apprenticeship; Leadership courses, development programs, technical courses, certification opportunities and more!

A collaborative and positive culture - join a diverse team of professionals that are as smart and driven as you.

A chance to make an impact – your work will contribute directly to our strategy.

Hybrid Work Environment - enjoy the flexibility of working from home and the energy of collaborating with peers in our dynamic offices.

20+ PTO days plus holidays and floating holidays in your first year.

Extensive medical, dental insurance and vision plan.

401K with corporate match, immediate vesting.

Health-and-wellness-related allowance programs.

Parental leave.

Tuition reimbursement.

Employee Stock Purchase Plan.

Employee Assistance Program.

Gartner Gives Charity Match.
And much more!

#LI-Hybrid
#LI-TW1

Who are we?

At Gartner, Inc. (NYSE:IT), we guide the leaders who shape the world.

Our mission relies on expert analysis and bold ideas to deliver actionable, objective business and technology insights, helping enterprise leaders and their teams succeed with their mission-critical priorities.

Since our founding in 1979, we’ve grown to 20,000 associates globally who support over 13,000 client enterprises in ~90 countries and territories. We do important, interesting and substantive work that matters. That’s why we hire associates with the intellectual curiosity, energy and drive to want to make a difference. The bar is unapologetically high. So is the impact you can have here.

What makes Gartner a great place to work?

Our vast, virtually untapped market potential offers limitless opportunities – opportunities that may not even exist right now – for you to grow professionally and flourish personally. How far you go is driven by your passion and performance.

We hire remarkable people who collaborate and win as a team. Together, our singular, unifying goal is to deliver results for our clients.

Our teams are inclusive and composed of individuals from different geographies, cultures, religions, ethnicities, races, genders, sexual orientations, abilities and generations.

We invest in great leaders who bring out the best in you and the company, enabling us to multiply our impact and results. This is why, year after year, we are recognized worldwide as a great place to work.

Gartner is the world authority on AI

At Gartner, you’ll join a company at the very center of the AI revolution. Gartner has proactive, objective guidance throughout clients’ AI journeys. We set the standard for how organizations leverage artificial intelligence to drive meaningful impact. You’ll have access to unmatched resources, expertise, and technology, and play a key role in helping Gartner and our clients innovate and grow as we leverage AI to transform business and technology landscapes.

It’s an exciting time to be at Gartner, with limitless opportunities to make a real impact, grow your skills, and build a lasting, meaningful career in a field that’s reshaping the way we operate. If you’re passionate about AI and want to be part of a team that’s guiding the leaders who shape the world, Gartner is the place for you.

What do we offer?

Gartner offers world-class benefits, highly competitive compensation and disproportionate rewards for top performers.

In our hybrid work environment, we provide the flexibility and support for you to thrive — working virtually when it's productive to do so and getting together with colleagues in a vibrant community that is purposeful, engaging and inspiring.

Ready to grow your career with Gartner? Join us.

Gartner believes in fair and equitable pay. A reasonable estimate of the base salary range for this role is 86,000 USD - 118,000 USD. Please note that actual salaries may vary within the range, or be above or below the range, based on factors including, but not limited to, education, training, experience, professional achievement, business need, and location. In addition to base salary, employees will participate in either an annual bonus plan based on company and individual performance, or a role-based, uncapped sales incentive plan. Our talent acquisition team will provide the specific opportunity on our bonus or incentive programs to eligible candidates. We also offer market leading benefit programs including generous PTO, a 401k match up to $7,200 per year, the opportunity to purchase company stock at a discount, and more.
The policy of Gartner is to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, citizenship status, age, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected status and to seek to advance the principles of equal employment opportunity.

Gartner is committed to being an Equal Opportunity Employer and offers opportunities to all job seekers, including job seekers with disabilities. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access the Company’s career webpage as a result of your disability. You may request reasonable accommodations by calling Human Resources at +1 (203) 964-0096 or by sending an email to ApplicantAccommodations@gartner.com .

jobs.summary: Responsibilities Senior Windows Endpoint Engineer The Endpoint Engineering team is responsible for the technologies and capabilities that deliver the end user experience to our global crew through a mix of both devices and software. We are seeking a...
jobs.description:
Responsibilities

Senior Windows Endpoint Engineer

The Endpoint Engineering team is responsible for the technologies and capabilities that deliver the end user experience to our global crew through a mix of both devices and software. We are seeking a motivated and experienced senior engineer passionate about delivering a great experience to users through modern endpoint management tools and practices. As a Senior Windows Endpoint Engineer, you will be responsible for designing, implementing, and optimizing Intune solutions, for migration from MECM and AD joined devices to Azure AD joined and Intune Managed. You will develop Intune policies utilizing existing Active Directory group policies; work closely with cross-functional teams, to architect and deliver robust, scalable, and secure solutions and participate in the development of user stories, sprint planning, and test case development / execution within an agile project environment.

Qualifications

**This is a hybrid role based in Wayne, Pennsylvania. Hybrid is defined as in-office Tues-Wed-Thurs. This position cannot be based remote and would require relocation for candidates currently living outside of PA**

In this Role you will:

Design and implement Microsoft Intune and MECM solutions that meet end user requirements. Create and manage Intune policies for application deployment, device compliance, and security configurations. Ensure seamless integration with other Microsoft 365 services.

Windows 10 and 11 Provisioning: Design and deploy Windows 10 and 11 provisioning processes using Intune Autopilot and MECM. Configure and manage Windows Update for Business policies. Develop and maintain standardized Windows 10 and 11 deployment images.

Active Directory Group Policy Management: Design and manage Group Policy Objects (GPOs) in Active Directory to enforce security and configuration policies.

Security and Compliance: Implement security best practices and compliance policies within Intune and MECM.

Documentation and Training: Create detailed documentation of Intune and MCEM configurations, Windows provisioning processes, and Group Policy settings.

Collaborate with the architecture team to ensure alignment with broader infrastructure goals. Following ITIL industry-standard processes and procedures.

Stay up to date with the latest technology trends and advancements in endpoint devices and support techniques.

Participate in special projects and performs other related duties as assigned.

?

What it Takes:

Undergraduate degree in a related field or an equivalent combination of training and experience

5 or more years of experience diagnosing and solving problems in a Windows environment. Expertise in MECM and Intune.

Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.

Excellent written and oral communication skills.

?

Specializations that’ll have an impact:

Relevant certifications such as Microsoft Certified Intune Expert.

Advanced knowledge of the Windows OS, Intune, Azure, and endpoint architecture

Ability to identify and implement automation opportunities to increase productivity and maintain high quality deployments.

Experience leading and mentoring staff, technical support organizations.

Experience with production readiness review process.

Ability to work under pressure and handle multiple problems simultaneously.

jobs.summary: Associate UX Designer – Client Platform Wealth Management Platforms Purchase, New York We are seeking an Associate UX Designer to join our Wealth Management Client UX team that supports self-directed and FA managed clients. In this role, you will help...
jobs.description: Associate UX Designer – Client Platform Wealth Management Platforms Purchase, New York We are seeking an Associate UX Designer to join our Wealth Management Client UX team that supports self-directed and FA managed clients. In this role, you will help design data-rich experiences that give clients a clear picture of their portfolios and account data. You will also design analytics tools to give users a deeper understanding of their finances. You’ll contribute across the end-to-end product design lifecycle, partnering closely with UX leads, researchers, product owners, and engineers. You will be focused on translating business requirements into intuitive interfaces using components from our design system. This is a hands-on role responsible for user journeys, screen flows, wireframes, prototypes and high-fidelity designs. This role is required to work in-office a minimum of 4 days a week to support teamwork and collaboration. About Morgan Stanley Morgan Stanley is a leading global financial services firm providing a wide range of investment banking, securities, investment management and wealth management services. The Firm’s employees serve clients worldwide including corporations, governments and individuals from more than 1,200 offices in 43 countries. Morgan Stanley is committed to helping its employees build meaningful careers and we strive to be a place for people to learn, achieve and grow. Department Overview In the Wealth Management division, we help people, businesses and institutions build, preserve, and manage wealth so they can pursue their financial goals Wealth Management (WM) Platforms manages industry-leading platforms, across all WM channels and client segments, to provide a unified digital experience, unlock growth, and deliver efficiencies for Advisors, Clients, and Institutions. WM Platforms consists of ten sub-teams including: Field Experience & Platforms, Digital Client Experience & Platforms, Workplace Platforms, Automation & Workflow, Digital Trading & Investing, Generative AI, UX Design & Research, Strategy & Execution, WM Platforms Risk, and the Chief Operating Office. Morgan Stanley Wealth Management’s User Experience (UX) team designs digital experiences that help clients understand their finances, monitor investments, and act with confidence across Morgan Stanley and E*TRADE from Morgan Stanley platforms. We design for two primary audiences: clients and Financial Advisors, who guide clients through important financial milestones We are a growing team of designers and researchers who bring an entrepreneurial mindset to a leading global financial firm. We use design thinking and a user-centered approach to solve real customer and business problems—often in complex, highly regulated environments. If you enjoy bringing clarity to complexity, focusing on the details, and building experiences that fit seamlessly into a large product ecosystem, we’d like to meet you. What you’ll do in the role: Partner with the UX lead and cross-functional teams to deliver experiences that balance user needs, business objectives, and platform constraints Translate complex business rules, edge cases, and user scenarios into simple screen flows and intuitive interface designs Create and iterate on design artifacts including journey maps, task flows, wireframes, high-fidelity mockups, and interactive prototypes Design within an established ecosystem by leveraging existing interaction patterns and design system components to maintain consistency across products Work closely with our research team to identify user pain-points and ideate ways to improve the product experience Deliver responsive/adaptive designs optimized for web and mobile, and collaborate with engineering to support accurate implementation (including specs and design QA) Demonstrate strong attention to detail—creating a strong visual hierarchy with typography and layout, as well as considering error handling and empty states Advocate for inclusive design by aligning work to accessibility expectations (e.g., WCAG/ADA) and supporting usability for a wide range of user abilities Use company-approved AI tools to concept, iterate and quickly prototype workflows Ask thoughtful questions, seek feedback, and continuously grow design craft and product understanding Articulate the rationale behind your design decisions Champion design system thinking across multiple tabs and products over isolated page level interactions Collaborate with UX designers on other teams to share ideas, provide feedback, align on common patterns Skills Required: You have an outstanding portfolio that demonstrates how you have created experiences that solved business and user needs You’ve spent 2-5 years as a UX designer who was a key contributor to the success of products that have launched You have a bachelor's degree in design or related field preferably, master’s degree a plus You have excellent skills in Figma You thrive in a collaborative, explorative environment and you value being on a diverse team to brainstorm and get feedback from within a large organization You practice active listening and contribute in-group situations, like brainstorms, audits, critiques, and whiteboard sessions You get excited by complex projects and can navigate a path forward when there is ambiguity You ask questions to clarify your understanding of the business rules, use cases and end users WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM MORGAN STANLEY: At Morgan Stanley, we raise, manage and allocate capital for our clients – helping them reach their goals. We do it in a way that’s differentiated – and we’ve done that for 90 years. Our values - putting clients first, doing the right thing, leading with exceptional ideas, committing to diversity and inclusion, and giving back - aren’t just beliefs, they guide the decisions we make every day to do what's best for our clients, communities and more than 80,000 employees in 1,200 offices across 42 countries. At Morgan Stanley, you’ll find an opportunity to work alongside the best and the brightest, in an environment where you are supported and empowered. Our teams are relentless collaborators and creative thinkers, fueled by their diverse backgrounds and experiences. We are proud to support our employees and their families at every point along their work-life journey, offering some of the most attractive and comprehensive employee benefits and perks in the industry. There’s also ample opportunity to move about the business for those who show passion and grit in their work. To learn more about our offices across the globe, please copy and paste https://www.morganstanley.com/about-us/global-offices into your browser. Expected base pay rates for the role will be between$58,000 and $115,000 per year at the commencement of employment. However, base pay if hired will be determined on an individualized basis and is only part of the total compensation package, which, depending on the position, may also include commission earnings, incentive compensation, discretionary bonuses, other short and long-term incentive packages, and other Morgan Stanley sponsored benefit programs. Morgan Stanley is an equal opportunity employer committed to building and maintaining a workforce that is diverse in experience and background. Our recruiting efforts reflect our strong commitment to a culture of inclusion, where individuals are hired, developed, and advanced based on their skills and talents. Our workforce reflects a broad cross-section of the global communities in which we operate, bringing a variety of backgrounds, talents, perspectives, and experiences. For more information, please visit: https://www.morganstanley.com/people-opportunities/eeo. At Morgan Stanley, we advise, originate, trade, manage and distribute capital for people, governments and institutions, always with a standard of excellence and guided by our core values. Morgan Stanley is dedicated to providing first-class service to our clients, in a way that reflects our commitment to creating a more sustainable future and fostering stronger communities around the world. In each line of business, we strive to demonstrate our belief in the power of transformative thinking, innovative strategies and leading-edge solutions—and in the ability of capital to work for the benefit of all society. What We Do

jobs.title: Lead Infrastructure Engineer - Public Cloud Storage
jobs.summary: About This Role Wells Fargo is seeking a Storage Engineer in the Public Cloud Storage space to lead the design, engineering, automation, and adoption of cloud-based storage services with a primary focus on Google Cloud Platform (GCP). This role will be...
jobs.description:
About This Role

Wells Fargo is seeking a Storage Engineer in the Public Cloud Storage space to lead the design, engineering, automation, and adoption of cloud-based storage services with a primary focus on Google Cloud Platform (GCP). This role will be responsible for developing cloud storage strategy, establishing enterprise standards, onboarding new technologies, and enabling application teams to seamlessly consume storage services across hybrid cloud environments.

The ideal candidate will possess strong experience designing and operating storage solutions across on-premises and public cloud platforms, enabling workload mobility and data services between enterprise data centers and cloud environments. This individual will serve as a technical leader, driving modernization, automation, governance, and operational excellence across the storage ecosystem.

In This Role, You Will

Design and implement scalable, secure, and resilient storage solutions in Google Cloud Platform (GCP)Develop and maintain enterprise storage standards, reference architectures, and engineering best practicesLead storage strategy initiatives supporting hybrid cloud and multi-cloud adoptionDrive onboarding of new storage products, services, and technologies.Partner with application, platform, security, and infrastructure teams to accelerate cloud adoptionDesign and implement workload migration strategies between on-premises and cloud environmentsBuild and maintain Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) solutions using Terraform.Automate storage provisioning, lifecycle management, and operational workflowsEstablish governance, security, compliance, encryption, and data protection standardsEvaluate emerging technologies and provide architectural recommendations to leadershipDevelop patterns for object, file, and block storage consumption across cloud and hybrid environmentsCollaborate with vendors and product teams on roadmap development and platform enhancementsSupport capacity planning, performance optimization, resiliency, disaster recovery, and cost management initiativesMentor engineers and provide technical leadership for cloud storage modernization efforts

Required Qualifications:

5+ years of Technology Infrastructure Engineering and Solutions experience, or equivalent demonstrated through one or a combination of the following: work experience, training, military experience, education5+ years of experience in storage engineering, cloud engineering, or infrastructure architectureDeep hands-on experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP)Experience designing and supporting:Cloud Object StorageCloud File StorageCloud Block StorageCloud Data ProtectionStrong experience implementing Infrastructure-as-Code using TerraformProven experience operating in hybrid cloud environments and migrating workloads between on-premises and cloud platformsExperience developing engineering standards, architecture patterns, and operational governance frameworksStrong understanding of cloud networking, security, identity, encryption, and compliance requirementsExperience leading large-scale enterprise onboarding and modernization initiativesExcellent troubleshooting, communication, and stakeholder management skillsDesired Qualifications:

Experience with Amazon Web Services (AWS)Experience with Microsoft AzureExperience operating within a regulated enterprise environmentExperience with Kubernetes and containerized storage solutionsExperience integrating storage services into CI/CD and platform engineering workflowsCloud certifications such as:Google Professional Cloud ArchitectGoogle Professional Cloud Network EngineerAWS Solutions ArchitectMicrosoft Azure ArchitectDesired Storage Platform Experience

Experience with any of the following technologies is highly desirable:

Public CloudGoogle Cloud Storage (GCS)FilestoreS3Persistent DiskMulti-cloud storage architecturesOn-PremisesNetApp ONTAPCohesityDell Technologies Storage Platforms

Key Skills

Cloud Storage ArchitectureHybrid Cloud StrategyTerraform / Infrastructure-as-CodePythonStorage Engineering (Block, File, Object)Cloud Governance & SecurityWorkload Migration & ModernizationAutomation & Platform EngineeringTechnical LeadershipVendor & Product ManagementStandards DevelopmentCost Optimization / FinOpsDisaster Recovery & Data Protection

Job Expectations:

This position is not eligible for visa sponsorship Establish the strategic direction for public cloud storage services.Deliver scalable, secure, and standardized storage platformsAccelerate cloud adoption through automation and self-service capabilitiesEnable seamless workload and data mobility across on-premises and cloud environmentsDrive innovation by evaluating and onboarding emerging storage technologiesImprove security, resiliency, operational efficiency, and customer experience across the enterprise storage landscape

Pay Range

Reflected is the base pay range offered for this position. Pay may vary depending on factors including but not limited to demonstrated examples of prior performance, skills, experience, or work location. Employees may also be eligible for incentive opportunities.

$119,000.00 - $206,000.00

Benefits

Wells Fargo provides eligible employees with a comprehensive set of benefits, many of which are listed below. Visit Benefits - Wells Fargo Jobs for an overview of the following benefit plans and programs offered to employees.

Health benefits401(k) PlanPaid time offDisability benefitsLife insurance, critical illness insurance, and accident insuranceParental leaveCritical caregiving leaveDiscounts and savingsCommuter benefitsTuition reimbursementScholarships for dependent childrenAdoption reimbursement

Posting End Date:

24 Jul 2026

Job posting may come down early due to volume of applicants.

We Value Equal Opportunity

Wells Fargo is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other legally protected characteristic.

Employees support our focus on building strong customer relationships balanced with a strong risk mitigating and compliance-driven culture which firmly establishes those disciplines as critical to the success of our customers and company. They are accountable for execution of all applicable risk programs (Credit, Market, Financial Crimes, Operational, Regulatory Compliance), which includes effectively following and adhering to applicable Wells Fargo policies and procedures, appropriately fulfilling risk and compliance obligations, timely and effective escalation and remediation of issues, and making sound risk decisions. There is emphasis on proactive monitoring, governance, risk identification and escalation, as well as making sound risk decisions commensurate with the business unit's risk appetite and all risk and compliance program requirements.

Applicants With Disabilities

To request a medical accommodation during the application or interview process, visit Disability Inclusion at Wells Fargo .

Drug and Alcohol Policy

Wells Fargo maintains a drug free workplace. Please see our Drug and Alcohol Policy to learn more.

Wells Fargo Recruitment And Hiring Requirements

Third-Party recordings are prohibited unless authorized by Wells Fargo. Wells Fargo requires you to directly represent your own experiences during the recruiting and hiring process.

jobs.title: IT Infrastructure Engineer I, II, III
jobs.summary: Job Title: IT Infrastructure Engineer I-III Ideal candidate statement:United Power is seeking an IT Infrastructure Engineer who sees end-user computing as a strategic platform. You understand that modern device management, built on cloud identity,...
jobs.description:
Job Title: IT Infrastructure Engineer I-III

Ideal candidate statement:United Power is seeking an IT Infrastructure Engineer who sees end-user computing as a strategic platform. You understand that modern device management, built on cloud identity, zero-trust principles, and intelligent automation, is how organizations enable their workforce while actually reducing complexity. You're excited about the architectural shift from domain-joined workstations to Intune-managed endpoints deployed through Autopilot, and you recognize both the technical innovation and the operational change that makes it real. You're equally at home reasoning through Group Policy and SCCM as you are thinking about self-service, user enablement, and the human side of transitioning to new tooling.This role sits at the intersection of EUC, cloud infrastructure, and cybersecurity. As part of the IT Infrastructure team, you'll work with Azure, servers, and VDI platforms while engaging closely with our security function to ensure defense is built into every layer-because you understand that modern infrastructure is only as strong as its architectural integrity. Above all, you bring vision about what's possible, paired with the patience and communication skills to bring others along.

Position Purpose and Objectives:

As an IT Infrastructure Engineer, you support the cooperative's vision and mission by providing reliable, high-performing IT infrastructure and services across the organization, including the OT environment. Reporting to the VP of IT Infrastructure and Operations, you are responsible for progressing, maintaining, and supporting IT & OT hyper-converged infrastructures, public cloud infrastructure, cybersecurity technologies & practices, implementing the disaster recovery program, and participating in assigned projects.

This position works with a diverse range of technologies across the focus areas of hybrid cloud infrastructure, network engineering, end user computing engineering, identity and access management, and systems administration. This is a hybrid position, primarily remote, but will require on-site work at any United Power location with little notice.

Your contributions will directly impact the organization's success, ensuring the stability, performance, and security of IT and OT infrastructures while delivering an excellent end-user and member experience.

Essential Functions and Responsibilities:

Engages in planning, design, implementation, administration and support activities for infrastructure technologies, integrations, cybersecurity, and disaster recovery across the focus areas of:o Hybrid Cloud Infrastructure

o Network Engineering

o End User Computing Engineering

o Identity and Access Management

o Systems Administration

Collaborates with vendors and managed service providers for the administration of infrastructure technologies and incident resolution.Documents designs, implementations, standards, and changes, and provides knowledge transfer to the IT team to ensure familiarity with the infrastructure environment.Applies and suggests improvements to standards, practices, and procedures for delivering infrastructure services.Proactively researches and recommends new technologies, tools, and approaches in each focus area to drive continuous improvement and innovation aligned with current and future business needs.Supports end-user experience by handling and documenting incidents and service requests related to infrastructure technologies in the ITSM platform.Participates in a cross-discipline team to perform regular cybersecurity functions, including review, discussion, and remediation of alerts, threats, and vulnerabilities.Engages in cyber incident response planning and functions and provides backup coverage for key cyber functions.Maintains working familiarity with all business functions, processes, systems, and aspects of the United Power technology environment.Ensures compliance with security policies, baselines, and best practices in the design, implementation, and maintenance of IT infrastructure and services.Creates, expands and maintains professional and productive working relationships with peers and stakeholders. Works collaboratively with colleagues and fosters a spirit of cooperation in the workplace.Communicates unresolved or highly visible issues to IT department leadership.Participates in a 24/7 on-call schedule within the department.Performs other such duties as may be requested or assigned to fulfill the needs of United Power in the interest of good management practice.Progression and Proficiency:

The IT Infrastructure Engineer class series consists of four positions: IT Infrastructure Engineer I, II, & III positions in the IT Infrastructure Engineer class series are flexibly staffed and based on demonstrating proficiency across the core skills and identified focus areas.

Proficiency levels are defined as:

Noviceo Possesses a basic understanding of tasks in this sphere and can perform them with some or no support in a relatively low-pressure environment.

Practitionero Demonstrates an advanced understanding of issues and topics in this sphere.

o Able to operate effectively under time constraints and pressure, with minimal or no assistance.

o Implements strategies and tactics to achieve goals and can adapt and improvise when necessary.

Experto Capable of operating effectively in conditions of uncertainty and stress.

o Willing and able to improve the skills of Novices when requested.

o Able to evaluate and implement new ideas proposed by Practitioners.

o Contributes to the development of best practices and standards within the sphere.

Ninjao Able to perform complex tasks in conditions of high uncertainty, stress, and conflict, within tight timeframes and with minimal or no mentorship.

o Demonstrates strong collaborative leadership skills and can effectively lead small teams.

o Innovates and introduces new ways of working, organizing, and teaching, and actively shares knowledge with those who are willing to learn.

o Recognized as a thought leader and expert within the organization for topics in this sphere.

Core Skills and Required Knowledge:

In reflection of the importance that United Power places on cybersecurity, all classifications in the series are expected to have familiarity with the CIS Critical Controls.Novice or higher proficiency level across all focus areas, with a minimum of one focus areas at the practitioner level or above.Demonstrated aptitude and enthusiasm for learning new technologies, with curiosity and ability to formulate questions when identifying and evaluating solutions or researching problems.Deliver exceptional customer service to peers and end-users, consistently demonstrating a strong commitment to customer satisfaction.Ability to work both independently and collaboratively, and as a member of a team.Possess exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal. Ability to determine the technical skill level of the information recipient, and to formulate and tailor communications accordingly.Ability to work independently based on general and specific direction, project task assignments, and departments' priorities. Day to day work is self-initiated and self-directed.Ability to organize and drive own daily activities based on both broad and specific directives, processes, and procedures, with ability to meet multiple deadlines, and effectively handle multiple tasks.Ability to collaborate with others to participate in root cause analysis and solve problems both within and outside area of technical expertise.Diagnose and resolve complex technical problems independently within areas of technical expertise.Demonstrated knowledge of and administration skills for the Microsoft 365 platform.Ability to explain the concepts of cloud computing, DNS, the OSI model, and virtual private networking to people with varying levels of technical knowledge.Focus Area: Hybrid cloud infrastructure

Hybrid cloud infrastructure combines on-premises hyper-converged solutions and public cloud resources to create a flexible and scalable computing environment.

Focus Area: Network Engineering

Network engineering involves designing, implementing, and maintaining the organization's network infrastructure to ensure reliable, secure, and efficient connectivity. Key skills include BGP, OSPF, SASE, VPN, MLAG, NGFW policy, wireless and cloud networking.

Focus Area: EUC (End User Computing) Engineering

EUC engineering focuses on designing, deploying, and managing the computing environments and devices used by end-users. Key skills include Microsoft Windows, Microsoft Azure, mobile device management, virtual desktop infrastructure, computer imaging, patch management, PowerShell, scripting, and application deployment packaging.

Focus Area: Identity and Access Management

Identity and Access Management (IAM) ensures the right individuals access the right resources at the right times for the right reasons. Key skills include hybrid Active Directory, SSO, OAuth, SAML, PKI, ADCS, NAC, FIDO2.

Focus Area: Systems Administration

Systems administration involves managing and maintaining the organization's server and application infrastructure ensuring the smooth operation of critical systems. Key skills include Windows & Linux administration, patching, backup & recovery, DNS, DHCP and email services.

Supervision Received and Exercised:

Receives both general and specific, guidance and direction from VP of IT Infrastructure & Operations, CIO, or IT project managers.

Education, Training and Experience:

IT Infrastructure Engineer I/II/III

Equivalent to graduation from a four-year college or university with major coursework in computer science, information systems or a closely related field. Equivalency may be demonstrated through a combination of training and progressively responsible experience that resulted in the required specialized knowledge and abilities to perform the assigned work in lieu of a degree.

Relevant advanced technical certifications, current training, actual work experience that is consistent with the job classifications and progression. Demonstrated experience as the technical lead for routine/small technical projects preferred.

IT Infrastructure Engineer II

AND combined 4 years' advanced, hands-on technical engineering and administration experience in technologies relevant to the position.

IT Infrastructure Engineer III

AND combined 8 years' advanced, hands-on technical engineering and administration experience in technologies relevant to the position.

Discretion/Latitude:

Works independently and as a member of the IT Infrastructure team, based on general and specific direction, Service Desk cases, project task assignments, department's priorities. Day to day work is self-initiated and self-directed based on priorities established by VP of IT Infrastructure & Operations, Project Managers, or CIO.

IT Infrastructure Engineer I / II

Ability to organize and drive own daily activities based on both broad and specific directives, processes and procedures, with ability to meet multiple deadlines, and effectively handle multiple tasks.

IT Infrastructure Engineer III

Ability to organize and drive own daily activities based on primarily on broad directives, processes and procedures, with ability to meet multiple deadlines, and effectively handle multiple tasks with little direct guidance.

Impact:

It is essential that the end users' and members' experience with the technology environment be positive, safe and productive. This position significantly contributes to that experience, and United Power's ability to deliver services to our members. This role is critical to the goal of delivering a reliable and high-performance infrastructure.

Liaison:

Works with all employees and external vendors.

Essential Physical & Mental Requirements:

Majority of time requires sitting, bending at neck, waist, legs, and arms; twisting...

jobs.title: Senior Digital Workplace Technician - USDS
jobs.summary: Washington D.C. Regular R&D - Security Job ID: A15629 Responsibilities About the Team The IT Support team's mission is to empower our organization with innovative technology solutions that enhance productivity, streamline operations, and drive growth. The...
jobs.description:
Washington D.C.
Regular
R&D - Security
Job ID: A15629

Responsibilities

About the Team The IT Support team's mission is to empower our organization with innovative technology solutions that enhance productivity, streamline operations, and drive growth. The IT Support team partners with cross-functional teams across the organization to deliver reliable technical support, guidance, and solutions. We are committed to collaboration, service excellence, and making technology work seamlessly for our employees. Through collaboration and a commitment to excellence, we aim to make technology work seamlessly for our users, ultimately enabling them to focus on what matters most – delivering value to our organization and our customers. About the Role The Sr. Digital Workplace Technician would be providing general hardware and software to business users and working with stakeholders to remove friction and increase staff productivity through the use of AI and automation. Due to the nature of the technical work, the candidate will be required to provide resolutions to tickets and service requests from FTEs, BPOs, TPAs either onsite at a TT USDS JV office location or remote via Lark and if needed Google Chat. Tasks and Responsibilities: - Site Ownership: Serves as the technical lead for onsite and regional offices, overseeing site visits, office expansions - Executive Cross-Functional Partnership: Acts as a professional IT ambassador, partnering with Engineering, Product, and Operations to align regional technology with global corporate objectives and AI-driven workflows. - Elite Technical Support (Tier 1-3): Provides top-notch, white-glove support for both onsite and remote teams, acting as the final escalation point for complex hardware and software troubleshooting. - Modern A/V & Live Stream Engineering: Owns the end-to-end setup and support of sophisticated A/V environments. Provides hands-on expertise with Logitech conference systems, Apple Mac Mini/iPad room controllers, Crestron control systems, and Lightware signal management. - Standardization & Documentation: Authors technical standards, security policies, and "Golden Image" best practices for global deployment, ensuring consistency across all regional sites. - Enterprise Service Management: Partners with service owners to evaluate and deploy enterprise solutions. Maintains Admin-level expertise across both Google Workspace and Microsoft Office 365 environments. - Infrastructure & Data Integrity: Manages lifecycle, backups, and monitoring for critical office infrastructure. Directs technical input for enterprise projects, emphasizing "Infrastructure as Code" (IaC) principles. - AI-Driven Efficiency & Automation: Proactively identifies operational gaps and builds solutions using AI tools (Gemini, Claude, ChatGPT) and automation platforms (Zapier, DevinAI) to eliminate manual tasks and improve user experience. - Lifecycle & Procurement: Manages onsite supply chains and procurement of high-end technical assets, ensuring the office is equipped with the latest verified technology. - Security & Compliance Champion: Maintains a high degree of awareness regarding cyber/information security, ensuring onsite hardware and networks adhere to Zero Trust frameworks.

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications - Advanced IT Foundation: Extensive direct user-facing support experience within a high-growth or FAANG/MAANG-equivalent environment. Expert-level troubleshooting across Windows OS, macOS, and Unix/Linux environments. - Education: College degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Computer Engineering, or a relevant technical discipline. - SaaS & Identity Mastery: Expert-level administration of Google Workspace and Microsoft Office 365. Deep familiarity with Google IAM, Active Directory, and Cloud-based identity providers. - UEM Expertise: Mastery of Omnissa Workspace One for multi-OS deployment (macOS, Windows, iOS, Android) and policy management. - Full-Stack Networking: Strong understanding of general networking (DNS, DHCP, SSL, OSI Model, TCP/IP) and hands-on familiarity with Cisco, Aruba, Palo Alto, and SDN technologies in MDF/IDF environments. - AI & LLM Proficiency: Demonstrated ability to utilize Gemini, Claude, ChatGPT, and DevinAI to write scripts, troubleshoot code, and automate workflows. Proficiency in Zapier for connecting disparate SaaS tools. Preferred Qualifications - Enterprise A/V Certifications: Relevant training or experience with Crestron programming or Lightware switching systems. - Advanced Networking/Security: Industry certifications such as CCNA, CCNP, MCSE, CompTIA Sec+, or CISSP. - Infrastructure Automation: Experience with Python, Bash, or PowerShell for automating system administration tasks. - Cloud Infrastructure: Hands-on experience with AWS, GCP, or Azure environments. - Virtualization: Familiarity with VMware Horizon VDI or similar cloud-desktop solutions. - Project Management: Proven experience managing large-scale IT office builds, moves, or global software migrations.

Job Information

【For Pay Transparency】 Compensation Description (Annually) - Washington, DC

The base salary range for this position in the selected city is $ 98640 - $ 163200 annually.

Compensation may vary outside of this range depending on a number of factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and experience, and location. Base pay is one part of the Total Package that is provided to compensate and recognize employees for their work, and this role may be eligible for additional discretionary bonuses/incentives, and restricted stock units.

Benefits may vary depending on the nature of employment and the country work location. Employees have day one access to medical, dental, and vision insurance, a 401(k) savings plan with company match, paid parental leave, short-term and long-term disability coverage, life insurance, wellbeing benefits, among others. Employees also receive 10 paid holidays per year, 10 paid sick days per year and 17 days of Paid Personal Time (prorated upon hire with increasing accruals by tenure).

The Company reserves the right to modify or change these benefits programs at any time, with or without notice.

About USDS

TikTok USDS Joint Venture LLC is dedicated to the safety and security of millions of Americans who create, discover, and connect with what they love on the apps we operate. The Joint Venture has been established in compliance with the Executive Order signed by President Trump on September 25, 2025. Our foundation is a comprehensive data privacy and cybersecurity program we operate under defined safeguards to protect national security and secure U.S. user data, apps and the algorithm. We safeguard the U.S. content ecosystem, holding decision-making authority for trust and safety policies and moderation. USDS Joint Venture helps ensure Americans can continue to express their creativity, discover new hobbies and interests, and build thriving communities and businesses on a global scale.

On-site presence across teams allows the company to operate with greater speed, alignment, and agility — especially in areas like real-time decision-making, team development, and integrated execution. As such, the company is shifting from a hybrid work model to a fully in-person schedule up to 5 days a week.

Why Join Us

Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.

We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.

Diversity & Inclusion

TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

USDS Reasonable Accommodation

USDS is committed to providing reasonable accommodations in our recruitment processes for candidates with disabilities, pregnancy, sincerely held religious beliefs or other reasons protected by applicable laws. If you need assistance or a reasonable accommodation, please reach out to us at https://tinyurl.com/USDS-RA

jobs.title: Director, Digital Workplace Platforms
jobs.summary: Are you a visionary leader with a passion for driving digital transformation? Do you have a deep understanding of technology and a track record of successfully implementing innovative solutions? If so, Applied Materials has an exciting opportunity for you...
jobs.description:
Are you a visionary leader with a passion for driving digital transformation? Do you have a deep understanding of technology and a track record of successfully implementing innovative solutions? If so, Applied Materials has an exciting opportunity for you to join our team as the Director of Digital Workplace Platforms. In this role, you will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive strategy to enhance our digital workplace and drive productivity, collaboration, and engagement across our global organization. We are seeking a dynamic individual with strong leadership skills, excellent communication abilities, and a demonstrated ability to manage complex initiatives. If you are ready to make a significant impact and shape the future of our digital workplace, we encourage you to apply today.

Develop and implement a comprehensive strategy for digital workplace platforms, in alignment with the company's overall goals and objectives.
Drive digital transformation across the organization, utilizing your strong understanding of technology and innovative thinking.
Lead and manage complex initiatives to enhance our digital workplace, including identifying and implementing new technologies, systems, and processes.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure the successful integration of digital workplace solutions.
Provide visionary leadership to inspire and motivate team members towards achieving digital workplace goals.
Communicate effectively with stakeholders at all levels of the organization, including executives, to gain buy-in and support for digital workplace initiatives.
Identify and assess potential risks and challenges related to digital workplace platforms, and develop mitigation strategies.
Monitor and analyze data to measure the effectiveness and impact of digital workplace solutions, and use this information to continually improve and optimize processes.
Stay informed about industry trends and advancements in digital workplace technology, and make recommendations for incorporating them into our strategy.
Foster a culture of collaboration, engagement, and productivity within the digital workplace, and ensure alignment with company values and culture.
Provide guidance and mentorship to team members, promoting their growth and development within the organization.
Collaborate with HR and other departments to ensure digital workplace solutions support employee needs and enhance the overall employee experience.

Applied Materials is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

We may ultimately pay more or less than the hiring range, and this range may be modified in the future. A candidate\'s position within the range may depend on competencies, education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, performance, shift, travel requirements, and organizational needs. This job is also eligible for an annual bonus incentive pay. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including paid time off, 11 holidays, medical/dental/vision insurance, a generous 401(k) match, lifestyle spending account, and other benefits to eligible employees. Note: No pay amount is considered wages or compensation until earned, vested, and determinable. Bonus, commission, or other compensation remains at the company\'s sole discretion unless paid and may be modified. Job Overview: This position leads the development and implementation of BCBSA\'s digital workplace strategy, focusing on enhancing productivity, collaboration, and user experience through technology in on-premise, cloud, and SaaS environments. It oversees design and delivery of digital solutions supporting a hybrid work environment, ensuring secure, seamless, and resilient technology experiences. The role collaborates with leaders to develop digital experience roadmaps, drive user adoption, improve digital engagement, and enable organizational success. Essential Functions include: Technology Innovation: Oversee integration and optimization of collaboration tools, endpoint management, AV solutions, and digital assistant technologies. Stay ahead of trends in digital employee experience, automation, and AI tools. Strategic Leadership: Define and drive the digital workplace vision, roadmap, and KPIs for a hybrid, digitally native workforce. In-office / Hybrid Experience: Create technology solutions for seamless in-person and remote collaboration, ensure AV reliability, provide a modern workplace, and support hybrid work. Strategic Communicator: Lead strategic communications emphasizing digital experience management to foster a digitally savvy workforce. Change Management: Lead adoption strategies, training, and communications for digital solutions, ensuring usability and adoption. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with HR, Facilities, Security, IT to align initiatives with the employee lifecycle. Security & Reliability: Ensure solutions meet security requirements and improve the digital workplace. People Leadership: Manage performance, foster growth, and create an inclusive environment, motivating and inspiring teams to achieve organizational goals. Qualifications: Preferred Bachelor\'s Degree or equivalent experience 12+ years of progressive leadership in IT, digital transformation, or workplace technology Knowledge in enterprise digital workplace delivery, platforms, strategic initiatives, stakeholder engagement, communication, leadership, and decision-making skills. Extra Posting information: This role aims to transform how employees interact with technology, ensuring every digital touchpoint enhances productivity, fosters collaboration, and empowers seamless, secure work. It involves leading vision, strategy, and execution of a modern digital workplace, delivering a resilient user experience. The leader partners with functional leaders to create experience roadmaps, adopting emerging technologies to shape the future of work. Budget oversight is up to $20M, with a team of 15+ external partners. #J-18808-Ljbffr

jobs.title: Sr Director Analyst, Digital Workplace Infrastructure and Operations (Remote-US)
jobs.summary: Senior Director, Analyst US-Remote About the role Gartner Analysts are industry thought leaders who create must‑have insights, market predictions and best practices for a broad range of world‑leading organizations. A Senior Director serves as a leader...
jobs.description:
Senior Director, Analyst

US-Remote

About the role
Gartner Analysts are industry thought leaders who create must‑have insights, market predictions and best practices for a broad range of world‑leading organizations. A Senior Director serves as a leader within Gartner’s Business and Technology Insights (BTI) group, establishing oneself as a credible voice within their designated market at local, regional and global levels. Utilizing exceptional research and analytical skills, a Senior Director plays a significant role in producing pragmatic and provocative insights which Gartner clients consume and apply to propel their business toward key objectives.

They are a trusted source of advice for clients, reinforcing Gartner’s value every day by engaging them via in‑person meetings, virtual meetings, sales support visits and Gartner conferences to discuss complex client challenges and offer appropriate recommendations.

In your role as the Senior Director Analyst for Digital Workplace Infrastructure and Operations, you will serve as a trusted advisor and recognized thought leader to senior IT executives, most notably Heads of Infrastructure & Operations (I&O), to help them navigate the evolution of the digital workplace in an era increasingly shaped by AI. You will engage with leaders who are responsible for balancing reliability, security, cost, and employee experience, helping them make informed decisions that align technology capabilities with business and workforce outcomes.

You will be primarily responsible for researching, analyzing, and advising Gartner clients on how the Head of I&O should design, govern, and continuously evolve their infrastructure and operations strategies to support a modern, hybrid digital workplace. This includes assessing operating models, service delivery approaches, tooling, and organizational capabilities required to scale effectively while managing risk and complexity. Your work will blend high‑level strategic perspective with pragmatic, experience‑driven guidance, translating emerging trends and markets, such as AI‑driven automation, digital employee experience improvements and modern endpoint management strategies into clear, actionable insights that Heads of I&O can apply within real‑world constraints.

What you will do
Create innovative, thought‑provoking, and highly leveraged “must‑have insights” content related to Head of I&O strategic priorities, digital workplace strategies, endpoint management markets, end‑user services planning, and employee experiences and productivity.

Share forward‑looking advice both in written research and in client‑facing presentations and interactions.

Create innovative, thought‑provoking, and highly leveraged “must‑have” insights grounded in real‑world I&O experience that address Head of I&O strategic priorities, including digital workplace strategy, endpoint management and device markets, end‑user services planning, and how AI‑driven technologies and operating models are reshaping employee experience, productivity, and operational efficiency.

Develop in‑depth analysis to identify the root cause of a client’s barriers or overall needs and reframe thinking to drive strategy forward.

Demonstrate thought leadership in establishing Insights positions across a team of analysts.

Bring provocative, independent insights to Gartner leaders that can evolve the course of a research agenda.

Research, analyze and predict market trends and shifts to provide clients and vendors with actionable insights.

Provide clients and prospects with actionable advice aligned to their designated content area via virtual or face‑to‑face interactions.

Create and deliver high‑value presentation materials on and off stage for Gartner events, industry and professional association conferences, and client briefings.

Support BTI and Sales: Provide sales support serving as voice of the market to help Insights teams create content and to drive engagement with clients to make progress against their critical priorities.

Provide high quality and timely content peer review.

Build credibility as an industry expert to represent Gartner insights, methodology and strategy.

Actively participate in innovation, ideation, and research discussions and collaborate effectively with peers in the Insights community.

Identify research process improvements or develop new processes that help the team and BTI provide excellent service delivery.

Be a mentor and a coach by supporting more junior team members.

Be client‑centric while actively seeking to help clients engage regularly and often with Gartner insights and interactions.

What you will need
Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience; Graduate degree preferred.

12+ years of relevant field or industry experience in one or more of the fo

jobs.title: End User Computing (EUC) Engineer
jobs.summary: Thank you for your interest in a career at Regions. At Regions, we believe associates deserve more than just a job. We believe in offering performance-driven individuals a place where they can build a career --- a place to expect more opportunities. If...
jobs.description:
Thank you for your interest in a career at Regions. At Regions, we believe associates deserve more than just a job. We believe in offering performance-driven individuals a place where they can build a career --- a place to expect more opportunities. If you are focused on results, dedicated to quality, strength and integrity, and possess the drive to succeed, then we are your employer of choice.

Regions is dedicated to taking appropriate steps to safeguard and protect private and personally identifiable information you submit. The information that you submit will be collected and reviewed by associates, consultants, and vendors of Regions in order to evaluate your qualifications and experience for job opportunities and will not be used for marketing purposes, sold, or shared outside of Regions unless required by law. Such information will be stored in accordance with regulatory requirements and in conjunction with Regions' Retention Schedule for a minimum of three years. You may review, modify, or update your information by visiting and logging into the careers section of the system.

Job Description:

At Regions, the End User Computing (EUC) Engineer is responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining desktop solutions that leverage various technologies and platforms. This role collaborates with cross-functional teams to understand business requirements, design scalable and secure architectures, and ensure a smooth deployment and operation of desktop environments.

Responsibilities

Designs end-to-end desktop solutions by analyzing business requirements, assessing existing infrastructure, and delivering scalable, secure, and high-performance architecturesDesigns and integrates application and desktop products, including virtual apps and desktops, to provide comprehensive and cohesive desktop solutionsConfigures and manages virtual machines, session hosts, virtual networks, and storage to create and maintain virtual desktop environmentsEnsures proper image management, user profile management, and application delivery mechanismsDesigns and configures network connectivity between on-premises infrastructure and cloud serves, including network security measures, such as firewalls, VPNs, and load balancers, to ensure secure and efficient data transferOptimizes desktop environments for performance, scalability, and user experienceMonitors and tunes resource allocation, load balancing, and network bandwidth to ensure optimal performanceImplements security measures, including data encryption, access controls, and compliance requirements, to protect desktop environments and ensure compliance with industry regulations and organizational policiesDesigns and implements high availability and disaster recovery solutions to minimize downtime and data lossCreates redundancy mechanisms, backup strategies, and failover mechanisms to ensure business continuityAssist with the set-up of monitoring tools and processes to proactively monitor desktop infrastructureIdentifies and troubleshoots performance issues, system errors, and network connectivity problems to maintain a stable and reliable environmentCreates design documentation, implementation guides, and operational procedures, policy documents and controlsLeads and coordinates with cross functional teams, to design and provide a timely resolution to all escalated issues related to the service. These cross functional teams include among others, support teams, agent owners, security and network teams and application SME'sWrites, tests, validates, and installs packages for software upgrades/patchesProvides support for incidents and problems escalated from our tier 1 and 2 support teams with a particular focus on PC hardware, operating systems, peripherals, and other issues
Requirements
Bachelor's degree in computer science, information technology, or a related field and four (4) years designing and implementing desktop solutionsOr High School Diploma/GED and eight (8) years designing and implementing desktop solutionsExperience in network design, including connectivity options, firewalls, VPNs, and load balancers
Preferences
Related certifications in A+, Azure, Citrix, Jamf, Microsoft technologies, etc.
Skills and Competencies
Ability to work with minimal supervisionExcellent customer service skillsFamiliarity with identity and access management conceptsKnowledge of operating systemsKnowledge of security best practices, data encryption, access controls, and compliance requirementsKnowledge of systems automation tools including PowerShell, VB scripting, or Shell scriptingKnowledge of typical server applications and services, (SCCM, SQL, Active Directory, WSUS, GPOs, Jamf, etc.)Knowledge of virtual desktop servicesStrong analytical, problem-solving, and conceptual skillsStrong verbal and written communications skillsUnderstanding of performance optimization techniques, monitoring tools, and troubleshooting methodologies
macOS & Jamf Pro Engineering Responsibilities
Implement and support Jamf Pro configurations including configuration profiles, policies, smart groups, inventory collection, and enrollment workflows.Assist with Zero-Touch Provisioning for macOS using Automated Device Enrollment (ADE) through Apple Business Manager.Apply and maintain macOS configuration baselines aligned with Bank security standards, CIS benchmarks, and MDM capabilities.Create and maintain macOS packages, scripts, and automation to support software deployment, patching, and endpoint configuration.Support integrations between Jamf Pro and enterprise systems such as identity providers, certificate services, and security platforms.Participate in macOS version lifecycle activities including testing, validation, phased deployments, and user communications.Leverage Jamf Pro APIs and built-in tooling to support reporting, remediation workflows, inventory accuracy, and configuration compliance.Develop and maintain scripts (bash, zsh, or Python) to assist with Jamf automation, macOS compliance checks, and provisioning tasks.
macOS Platform Knowledge
Demonstrated working knowledge of macOS system behavior including permissions, FileVault, MDM concepts, and security features.Experience using Apple Business Manager capabilities such as ADE and VPP for application deployment and device lifecycle support.Support macOS identity and authentication integrations, including certificates, SSO, and modern authentication methods.Stay current with Apple platform updates and new capabilities, contributing recommendations to improve reliability, security, and user experience.
Troubleshooting & Operational Support
Serve as an escalation resource for macOS and Jamf Pro issues requiring advanced troubleshooting beyond standard support queues.Collaborate with security, infrastructure, and identity teams to support standardized macOS endpoint management across the Bank.Contribute to engineering documentation including build procedures, configuration standards, deployment guides, and operational runbooks.
Preferred macOS / Jamf Experience
Jamf certification at the 200 or 300 level (or equivalent hands-on experience).Experience supporting macOS devices in enterprise or regulated environments.Familiarity with macOS security tooling such as Jamf Protect, EDR platforms, certificate services, and OS hardening practices.Exposure to integrating macOS into environments with Windows-centric tooling (e.g., Intune Conditional Access, Microsoft Defender).
Regions will not sponsor applicants for work visas for this position at this time. Applicants for this position must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

This position is intended to be onsite, now or in the near future. Associates will have regular work hours, including full days in the office three or more days a week. The manager will set the work schedule for this position, including in-office expectations. Regions will not provide relocation assistance for this position, and relocation would be at your expense. The locations available for this role are Birmingham, AL.

This position may be filled at a higher level depending on the candidate's qualifications and relevant experience.

Position Type
Full time

Compensation Details

Pay ranges are job specific and are provided as a point-of-market reference for compensation decisions. Other factors which directly impact pay for individual associates include: experience, skills, knowledge, contribution, job location and, most importantly, performance in the job role. As these factors vary by individuals, pay will also vary among individual associates within the same job.

The target information listed below is based on the Metropolitan Statistical Area Market Range for where the position is located and level of the position.

Regions offers a benefits package that is flexible, comprehensive and recognizes that "one size does not fit all" for benefits-eligible associates.
