jobs.title: Federal Solutions Engineer
jobs.summary: About the Role We are looking for a sharp, trusted, and autonomous Federal Solutions Engineer to support NinjaOne’s expansion into the Public Sector. As an embedded technical partner to our Federal Growth team, you’ll play a critical role in both...
jobs.description:
About the Role

We are looking for a sharp, trusted, and autonomous Federal Solutions Engineer to support NinjaOne’s expansion into the Public Sector. As an embedded technical partner to our Federal Growth team, you’ll play a critical role in both pre-sales strategy and post-sale onboarding, acting as a key translator between technical buyer needs and the broader Product and Engineering teams. This role is perfect for someone who thrives in mission-critical moments, loves navigating complex RFPs and government requirements, and can tailor messaging to both deep technical evaluators and high-level decision makers. You’ll serve as a customer advocate internally while building scalable tools, templates, and processes to support the next generation of federal growth.

Location - This is a Remote role open to candidates within Virginia or Maryland or those willing to relocate unassisted.

Travel Requirement - Up to 40% representing NinjaOne at key industry events, product launches, and customer engagements.

What You’ll Be Doing

Own the technical presales experience for Federal and SLED accounts, guiding customers through evaluation, demo, architecture design, and onboarding phases.

Deliver tailored product demos and technical deep dives that align with customer security standards, compliance needs (FedRAMP, DoD IL2/4/5), and procurement requirements.

Support and guide the implementation of the NinjaOne platform for new public sector customers—ensuring quick time-to-value and customer success.

Act as the technical translator between Sales and Product—surfacing actionable feedback and feature gaps that directly impact deal velocity or retention.

Assist with RFPs, RFIs, and security questionnaires, providing accurate, compliance-aligned technical responses that differentiate NinjaOne’s value.

Troubleshoot and resolve customer technical issues, including endpoint configuration, agent behavior, networking challenges, and integrations.

Collaborate with Engineering and Product Management on solution enhancements that improve the Federal customer experience.

Create and maintain reusable content and lab environments to scale future engagements (demos, battlecards, scripts, templates, etc.).

Represent NinjaOne at industry events, partner summits, and key government-facing conferences as a technical ambassador.

Technical Requirements

Proficiency with Windows environments (registry, services, performance troubleshooting).

Working knowledge of Linux and macOS systems and basic command-line troubleshooting.

Solid foundation in networking and security concepts, including firewalls, VPN, DNS, and common endpoint protections.

Familiarity with Active Directory, remote access tools, and endpoint deployment methodologies.

Hands-on experience with RMM tools, ITSM platforms, and endpoint lifecycle management is highly preferred.

Ability to quickly learn and adapt to new technologies and translate their value to customers.

Experience using Salesforce, Jira, Highspot, or equivalent systems is required.

About You

5+ years in a Sales Engineer, Solutions Architect, or Technical Consultant role—within the Public Sector Enterprise space.

Exceptional communicator—capable of clearly articulating complex information to both technical and non-technical audiences, from system administrators to procurement and leadership.

A calm and credible presence in high-stakes, high-pressure customer conversations.

Customer-first mindset—you genuinely care about their experience and success.

Organized, self-directed, and capable of thriving in a remote-first, fast-paced environment.

Familiarity with FedRAMP, DoD Impact Levels, and NIST frameworks is required.

You align with our values Curiosity, Integrity, Kindness, Humility, Builders and are committed to creating an environment where these values flourish in our teams.

Bachelor’s degree preferred but not required; respected technical certifications (e.g., Security+, MCSE, CISSP) is preferred.

Ability to obtain a security clearance is required.

About Us

NinjaOne automates the hardest parts of IT to deliver visibility, security, and control over all endpoints for more than 20,000 customers. The NinjaOne automated endpoint management platform is proven to increase productivity, reduce security risk, and lower costs for IT teams and managed service providers. NinjaOne is obsessed with customer success and provides free and unlimited onboarding, training, and support. NinjaOne is #1 on G2 in endpoint management, patch management, remote monitoring and management, and mobile device management.

What You’ll Love

We are a collaborative, kind, and curious community.

We honor your flexibility needs with full-time work that is hybrid remote.

We have you covered with our comprehensive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, and vision insurance.

We help you prepare for your financial future with our 401(k) plan.

We prioritize your work-life balance with our unlimited PTO.

We reward your work with opportunity for growth and advancement.

Additional Information

This position is NOT eligible for Visa sponsorship.

Starting pay for the successful applicant depends on a variety of job-related factors, including but not limited to location, market demands, experience, job-related knowledge, and skills. The benefits available for this position include medical, dental, vision, 401(k) plan, life insurance coverage and PTO. For roles based in Maryland, the base salary hiring range for this position is $140,000 to $140,000 per year with On Target Earnings of $200,000 to $200,000 per year.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, marital status, veteran status, or any other status protected by applicable law. We are committed to providing an inclusive and diverse work environment.

jobs.summary: About the position We are seeking a Senior/Lead Endpoint Security Engineer (Developer) to help implement, automate, operate, and continuously improve endpoint security capabilities across the enterprise. In this role, you will work hands-on with...
jobs.description:
About the position

We are seeking a Senior/Lead Endpoint Security Engineer (Developer) to help implement, automate, operate, and continuously improve endpoint security capabilities across the enterprise. In this role, you will work hands-on with CrowdStrike to help ensure Salesforce endpoints are protected against threats impacting the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of enterprise systems. You will contribute to delivering a consistent, risk-based, and standards-aligned endpoint security posture, supporting endpoints across macOS, Windows, Linux, and mobile platforms. This is a hands-on engineering role focused on execution, reliability, and scale. You will collaborate closely with architects, senior engineers, product managers, and partner teams to deploy and operate endpoint security solutions, automate workflows, and improve detection and response capabilities as the environment evolves. You'll be a part of a broader team with deployment, automation and operational responsibilities across the full spectrum of endpoint security, including endpoint hardening, malware and virus detection, SaaS Security Posture Management (SSPM), Identity Protection (IDP) and other CrowdStrike capabilities.

Responsibilities

Develop and support endpoint security using CrowdStrike across: Endpoint Detection & Response (EDR) SSPM Vulnerability scanning and posture management CrowdStrike EM/VM module Identity ProtectionBuild Deployment AutomationHelp ensure Enterprise-wide deployment of endpoint security tools, achieving high coverage by aligning tool capabilities to the enterprise device inventory (including rollout of agents across macOS, Windows, Linux, and mobile platforms).Engage in threat modeling and participate in vendor evaluationAssist in proof-of-concepts (PoCs) for new technologies that align with evolving security and business needsContribute to platform enhancements and design decisionsSupport the definition and enforcement of policies, SOPs, and operational protocols for endpoint security tools by developing automation and related utilities.Help ensure that endpoint hygiene meets or exceeds regulatory and compliance requirements.Design and actively participate in the implementation of robust automation pipelines—leveraging scripting (Python) —to reduce manual effort, enforce consistency, and support rapid incident response.Work closely with security engineers contributing towards the detection logic & response workflows, collaborate with SIEM, SOAR, and telemetry teams to integrate and tune event ingestion, alerting, and remediation across multiple systems.Develop and maintain scripts (Python) and automation to: Reduce manual operational effort Improve consistency of endpoint configurations Support faster remediation and response workflowsContribute to CI/CD, infrastructure-as-code and configuration-as code efforts related to endpoint security services.Partner with Product Management throughout the entire project lifecycle, from initial design and vendor selection to implementation and ongoing operations, ensuring projects are delivered on time and with high quality.Develop and maintain comprehensive documentation for security services, policies, and procedures.Stay current with the latest threats and technologies in the cloud security and endpoint security landscape, actively seeking opportunities to innovate and improve our security jobs.title: Staff Windows Low Level C++ Engineer - Endpoint security
jobs.summary: Our Purpose At SentinelOne, we are driven by a clear purpose: to give the advantage to those who secure our future. As AI reshapes how organizations build, operate, and innovate, the responsibility to protect them becomes more critical than ever. When you...
jobs.description:
Our Purpose

At SentinelOne, we are driven by a clear purpose: to give the advantage to those who secure our future. As AI reshapes how organizations build, operate, and innovate, the responsibility to protect them becomes more critical than ever. When you join SentinelOne, your work helps protect global enterprises, critical infrastructure, and the technologies shaping tomorrow. If you are motivated by meaningful challenges and want your impact to be real, measurable, and global, you will find purpose here.

About Us

SentinelOne is a company at the intersection of AI and security, pioneering a new operating model for cybersecurity. Our AI-native platform unifies protection across endpoint, cloud, identity, data, and AI systems to deliver autonomous detection and response with clarity and speed. By combining real-time analytics, intelligent automation, and a unified data foundation, we reduce noise, simplify complexity, and empower security teams to focus on what truly matters.

Our teams are builders, problem-solvers, and innovators committed to shaping the future of security. If you are excited to solve hard problems alongside talented, mission-driven people, we invite you to help us build a safer future for humanity.

What Are We Looking For?
We're looking for people who are relentlessly curious and committed to continuous learning. AI is reshaping every function across our business, and we enable every team member, regardless of role or level, to build fluency in AI tools and concepts. Those who thrive here actively seek out new solutions, experiment thoughtfully, and apply what they learn to drive better, faster, smarter outcomes.

As a Windows Low Level Engineer - Endpoint security, you will design and develop advanced security features for SentinelOne's Windows endpoint protection platform, operating across both user-mode and kernel-mode environments. You will build and optimize mission-critical components including anti-tampering protections, network sensors, and agent infrastructure while solving complex performance and stability challenges at massive scale. By researching Windows internals and debugging deep system issues, you will collaborate with distributed engineering teams to deliver high-impact security capabilities protecting millions of devices worldwide.

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Software Engineer with a strong background in low-level C++ programming and a deep understanding of Windows OS, to help us in developing our Endpoint Protection product, the core part of our business, and to join one of our Platform group teams. (We're currently growing multiple teams and are open to new colleagues with a wide variety of backgrounds within Windows development!).

The Platform group teams are in charge of some of our most complex low-level areas, such as:

Windows agent's Anti-Tampering (self-protection from malware attempting to harm the agent), network sensors, device control, and other kernel-side features involving both research and development,
usermode security & agent infrastructures - e.g. installers, logging, services such as communication layer & interfaces for our Windows agent, for improving the quality, performance & stability of the agent and making the user experience smoother.

By joining our group, you will collaborate with talented engineers who are leading a feature through the whole development lifecycle. Your work will have an impact on millions of endpoints in one of the mainstream areas of the EPP/EDR agent of SentinelOne - a product recognized 5x-in-a-row as Gartner MQ Leader, scoring highly in many MITRE Att&ck assessments, valued by users in Gartner Peer Insights and more.

What will you do?

As a new member of one of our distributed development teams, you will work in both user-mode & kernel-mode, and deal with a large variety of fields.
You will have the chance to design, develop primarily in modern C++ (23), test and debug the most complicated real-world problems (often unseen before). In order to successfully deal with this challenge, you will continuously enrich your knowledge and experience with Windows OS internals & Kernel, Endpoint Protection, and Endpoint Detection & Response eco-system.
Our teams design and execute significant new features, as well as rewrite and reimplement the existing ones from scratch. You will also deal with extraordinary performance & optimization challenges, given the scale of our cybersecurity SW (handling hundreds of billions of events every day).
Your work will influence the security of tens of millions of Windows devices, that we protect at enterprise & government customers across the globe (incl. 4 of Fortune 10 and hundreds of Global 2000).
You'll also be encouraged to share knowledge within both Platform teams and the broader Windows group.

What skills & knowledge should you bring?

Multiple years of experience as a software engineer in the area of lowlevel programming using modern C++ (or using C and a willingness to learn modern C++)
Hands-on experience and in-depth understanding of Windows OS design & internals, Windows low-level development, with debugging of Windows applications and familiarity with related standard debugging and profiling tools
Previous experience working on a production-grade product with a wide-scale deployment
Great communication skills and the ability to work both independently and with peers from different time zones, problem-solving and out-of-the-box thinking
Attention to detail and high coding standards
An advantage: experience with Windows Kernel and Windows Drivers development, experience in security-oriented development, with reversing or malware analysis

Why SentinelOne?
AI is redefining how the world operates and rewriting the rules of security in real time, and SentinelOne was built for this moment. From day one, we architected an AI-native platform designed to operate at machine speed, not as an add-on to legacy systems but as the foundation itself. If you want to build where innovation and impact move together, this is that place.

We invest in our Sentinels with comprehensive, competitive benefits designed to support you and your family:

Equity & Rewards

Restricted Stock Units (RSUs)
Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)

Time Off & Wellbeing

Competitive leave benefits
Gender-neutral parental leave

Insurance & Financial Security

Medical and insurance benefits
Pension

Work Perks & Flexibility

Global home office allowance

Wellness & Lifestyle

Wellbeing allowance
MultiSport benefit program
SentinelOne is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

SentinelOne participates in the E-Verify Program for all U.S. based roles.

jobs.description:
Desktop Engineer

100% Remote

6-month Contract to Hire

Our client, an IT Solutions Client is looking for an experienced Desktop Engineer to join their growing team. These Desktop Engineers will work to support a large user base of about 11,000 people. This position is focused in Intune, Autopilot and O365 skills.

This position is 100% remote, EST. Candidates MUST convert after a 6 month contract period.

Summary:

Microsoft Desktop Engineers are responsible for designing, deploying, and managing enterprise-wide Windows OS endpoint solutions. This role is pivotal in ensuring the security, performance, and reliability of all corporate endpoint devices including laptops, desktops, and virtual endpoints are meeting or exceeding business demand. This role requires technical knowledge of endpoint management platforms and a passion for delivering scalable and secure solutions with a focus on user-friendly experiences.

Key Responsibilities:

Endpoint Management & Deployment (Microsoft Intune)

Manage and optimize tools such as Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune), Entra ID, and additional related Microsoft solutions.
Administer tailored Windows Autopilot solution(s) for clients with accompanying Intune policy dictation.
Create and implement device compliance policies, configuration profiles, and PowerShell scripted solutions to ensure endpoint security and governance.
Manage and optimize Windows update policies, application packaging, and deployment.
Install, upgrade, support and troubleshoot various hardware and applications hosted on a Windows Operating System to meet/exceed Enterprise needs.

Virtual Desktop Administration

Microsoft Windows 365 (Cloud PC) deployment, administration, and reporting.
Amazon Workspace deployment, administration, and reporting.

Monitor, analyze, and troubleshoot endpoint issues leveraging tools like Log

Analytics and Endpoint Analytics.

Collaborate with IT security, infrastructure, and service desk teams to improve the

end-user experience and reduce support incidents.

Maintain documentation, standard operating procedures, and knowledge base

articles related to device provisioning and endpoint management.

Qualifications:

Experience:

2+ years of hands-on experience with Microsoft Intune, Autopilot, and endpoint management concepts.
2+ years of experience with enterprise security practices for endpoint devices.
Strong knowledge with Microsoft Entra IT (formerly Azure AD), Group Policy concepts, and MDM policies.
Expert understanding of Windows client operating systems (Windows 10/11), Office 365, and modern management principles.

Technical Skills:

Proficiency in PowerShell scripting and task automation for endpoint management.
Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills with a user-first mindset.
Strong comprehension of MS Security Patching process.
Strong comprehension of MS Office 365 deployment/administration.
Strong MS Office Suite application usage skillset.

Soft Skills:

Excellent communication and documentation skills.

Nice to Have:

Professional Certifications a plus: Microsoft Modern Desktop Administrator, MSCE, AWS Solutions Architect, Network+, A+, etc.

Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field; or equivalent experience.
Experience with Zero Trust architecture and modern endpoint security approaches.
Experience and knowledge with ManageEngine (Endpoint Central)
Experience and knowledge with Qualys VMDR, Cisco Endpoint Secure, and CrowdStrike
ITIL or other service management frameworks.
Experience and knowledge with ServiceNow and Freshservice (Freshworks)
Firm understanding of network infrastructure and protocols.

jobs.description:
Infrastructure Engineer - Security Architecture - 18781 - Bloomberg

Skip to content

Bloomberg

Our CompanyEventsSearch JobsLoginInfrastructure Engineer - Security Architecture

Location

New York

Business Area

Engineering and CTO

Ref #

10050604

Description & Requirements

Our Team:

The Global Corporate Technology Group is responsible for designing, deploying, and supporting Bloomberg's enterprise technology systems used by employees across our global environment. Within that organization, the Security Architecture team engineers and operates endpoint security services used across the corporate environment.

Our team owns the design, build, deployment, integration, and support of endpoint security technologies across Windows and macOS. Linux support is expected to grow over time. We support Enterprise Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) and Antivirus (AV) products including Carbon Black and CrowdStrike, Endpoint Privilege Management (EPM), and the integrations needed to connect these services with internal identity, logging, and software deployment systems.

We are responsible for agent deployment, approved control rollout, version upgrades, automation, staged release, rollback planning, health monitoring, and production support. Endpoint stability and performance are a priority for the team. We work closely with internal Corporate Technology teams including Endpoint User Compute, Chief Technology Office (CTO), RISK, Technology Vulnerability Management (TVM), and the Computer Incident Response Team (CIRT) and Security Operations Center (SOC), but our focus is engineering, integration, and service ownership.

The Role:

As an Infrastructure Engineer, you will build, automate, deploy, and operate endpoint security services at scale. This is an engineering role focused on automation, integrations, and production ownership, not a click-ops administration role. You will take business and security requirements and turn them into technical designs, rollout plans, implementation tasks, test plans, runbooks, and support procedures, then execute those plans in production.

The work is centered on endpoint technologies. That includes automating configuration changes through vendor Application Programming Interfaces (APIs), staging rollouts through identity and device targeting, collecting agent and console logs into internal systems, and managing upgrades with clear validation and rollback. Endpoint performance, user impact, and service health must be considered in every change.

This role requires strong infrastructure engineering fundamentals, experience operating at enterprise scale, and the ability to apply a modern Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) to endpoint security services.

We'll trust you to

Take business and security requirements and turn them into technical designs, rollout plans, implementation tasks, test plans, runbooks, and support models, then execute those plans in productionDesign, implement, and operate endpoint security services across Carbon Black, CrowdStrike, CyberArk Endpoint Privilege Management (EPM), Sysmon, and related internal integrationsBuild and maintain automation using Python, PowerShell, Bash, Ansible, Terraform, and vendor or internal Application Programming Interface (API) integrations to provision, configure, validate, upgrade, and troubleshoot endpoint agents at scaleBuild and manage Git based workflows and Continuous Integration and Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) pipelines for endpoint service configuration and automation, including Pull Request (PR) review, automated validation, staged promotion, rollback, and controlled release practicesPlan and execute agent deployments, upgrades, and approved control rollout with clear validation around endpoint performance, user impact, service health, and rollback safetyBuild observability for the services you own, including health checks, dashboards, alerting, telemetry validation, and operational reporting, with an understanding of Service Level Objectives (SLOs) and Service Level Indicators (SLIs)Troubleshoot and restore service issues involving authentication, networking, proxies, certificates, endpoint agents, logging pipelines, and Software as a Service (SaaS) to on premise integrations, while partnering with internal teams and vendors to drive long term fixesYou'll need to have

4+ years of experience engineering and operating enterprise infrastructure or endpoint security services in production at scale across Software as a Service (SaaS) and on premise environmentsExperience in Python or another object oriented language, plus PowerShell or Bash for operational automationExperience building tools and integrations using Representational State Transfer (REST) APIs and working with enterprise infrastructure services and vendor technologiesExperience with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and orchestration frameworks such as Terraform, Ansible, or similar toolsStrong familiarity with Git workflows, Pull Request (PR) review, automated testing, and Continuous Integration and Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) pipelinesStrong troubleshooting skills across authentication and authorization flows, enterprise networking, proxy constrained environments, certificates, endpoint agents, and distributed dependenciesExperience building and operating observability for enterprise services, including health metrics, dashboards, alerting, telemetry checks, and operational measurements tied to SLOs and SLIsThe ability to take ownership of issues end to end, create clear technical documentation from requirements, and drive work through implementation, validation, and supportWe'd love to see

Experience with one or more of the following technologies: Carbon Black, CrowdStrike, CyberArk Endpoint Privilege Management (EPM), Microsoft Entra, or SysmonExperience managing endpoint agent lifecycle activities at scale, including deployment, approved control rollout, health validation, upgrades, rollback, and performance monitoringExperience integrating SaaS endpoint services with internal identity, logging, or software deployment systemsExperience building drift detection, closed loop validation, or self-healing automation for endpoint servicesStrong written and verbal communication skills and the ability to work independently in a production engineering environmentSalary Range = 130000 - 225000 USD Annually + Benefits + Bonus

The referenced salary range is based on the Company's good faith belief at the time of posting. Actual compensation may vary based on factors such as geographic location, work experience, market conditions, education/training and skill level.

We offer one of the most comprehensive and generous benefits plans available and offer a range of total rewards that may include merit increases, incentive compensation (exempt roles only), paid holidays, paid time off, medical, dental, vision, short and long term disability benefits, 401(k) +match, life insurance, and various wellness programs, among others. The Company does not provide benefits directly to contingent workers/contractors and interns.

Discover what makes Bloomberg unique - watch our podcast series for an inside look at our culture, values, and the people behind our success.

jobs.description:
We take pride in our culture and strive to make Bobcat - GDN a place where people want to work, achieve excellence, remain curious and humble, and build lifelong relationships.

Access & Endpoint Administrator

The Access & Endpoint Administrator is responsible for the full lifecycle management of employee IT access and equipment, including onboarding, offboarding, role changes, and endpoint provisioning. This role ensures employees have the appropriate systems access, hardware, and profiles to be productive from day one while maintaining security and accuracy throughout the employee lifecycle.

This position also provides secondary support to the Help Desk by assisting with ticket resolution, troubleshooting, and end-user support as needed.

Access & Endpoint Administrator

Salary $60,000-$70,000

Monday -Friday (No Weekends) Onsite position

Great Benefits

Responsibilities

Onboarding, Offboarding, and Lifecycle Management

Coordinate and execute all IT onboarding activities for new employees, including account creation, hardware provisioning, and access setup.Manage IT offboarding processes by promptly deactivating accounts, revoking access, and coordinating hardware return.Process employee job changes, promotions, and transfers by updating system access, group memberships, and endpoint profiles.Create, maintain, and terminate end-user accounts based on authorized requests, including access changes and password resets.Maintain accurate endpoint and user profile information within Endpoint Management systems.Identity, Access, and Systems/Hardware Administration

Image, configure, and deploy laptops, desktops, and mobile devices.

Prepare and stage hardware prior to employee start dates.

Manage hardware inventory and track assignments throughout the device lifecycle.

Troubleshoot hardware issues and coordinate repairs or replacements as needed.

Administer user accounts and access using Azure Active Directory (Entra ID).

Manage devices and user profiles using Intune / Endpoint Manager and AutoPilot.

Apply and manage Group Policies and configuration profiles.

Support Single Sign-On (SSO) integrations and assist with API or webhook-based provisioning where applicable.

Help Desk & End User Support

Respond to Help Desk tickets via phone, email, IM, remote support tools, and in person.

Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to:

PC hardware and software

Office phones

Printers, copiers, and scanners

Mobile devices and peripherals

Log all work in the ticketing system and ensure tickets are resolved or escalated appropriately.

Monitor and manage assigned tickets, providing timely updates and ensuring user satisfaction.

Follow documented IT processes and contribute to continuous improvement of onboarding and support workflows.

Handle additional IT support duties as assigned.

Qualifications

Experience with:

- Azure Active Directory (Entra ID)

- Intune / Endpoint Manager

- AutoPilot

- Group Policies

Experience with hardware setup, imaging, device management, as well as access creation and tracking.

Strong, meticulous attention to detail and ability to create & follow standardized processes.

Clear communication skills.

Experience in creating concise, scalable procedures and SOP documents.

Strong organizational skills with the ability to multitask and prioritize in a fast-paced environment undergoing significant growth.

Self-starter capable of working independently and collaboratively.

Experience with Single Sign-On (SSO), APIs, and webhooks.

Prior experience supporting employee onboarding/offboarding processes.

Familiarity with IT ticketing systems, remote access software and endpoint inventory management.

- Experience with Atera & AnyDesk is a plus.

Work Schedule & Location

Monday–Friday between the hours of 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM

On-site position located in Valley Park, MO

We are always on the lookout for people who bring fresh perspective and life experiences to our team.

jobs.summary: Your Impact At Goldman Sachs, Workplace Engineering is at the forefront of driving our firm's adoption of cutting-edge technologies for productivity, communications, and collaboration. As a Senior Technical Vice President, you will play a pivotal...
jobs.description:
Your Impact

At Goldman Sachs, Workplace Engineering is at the forefront of driving our firm's adoption of cutting-edge technologies for productivity, communications, and collaboration. As a Senior Technical Vice President, you will play a pivotal leadership role in shaping the strategic direction, architecture, and operational excellence of our Microsoft 365 platforms. Your vision and leadership will directly influence how our global workforce interacts, collaborates, and innovates, ensuring robust, secure, and scalable solutions that underpin our business objectives and client engagement. You will lead a high-performing engineering organization, driving innovation in web, mobile, social, collaboration, and user experience, fundamentally changing the way we operate across the firm.

Role Summary

This role is a Microsoft 365 Engineer with a core focus on Microsoft 365 engineering, Intune (mobile application and device management), M365 platform readiness, extensibility, security, and enterprise-scale adoption. We are seeking a visionary and experienced technical leader to serve as our Senior Technical Vice President, overseeing the strategic planning, engineering, and governance of our enterprise-wide Microsoft 365 ecosystem. This role demands a deep technical understanding of M365 services, coupled with exceptional leadership capabilities to guide global teams, define architectural standards, and drive the adoption of advanced features, including AI-powered capabilities like Copilot, the Teams suite, and the Power Platform. You will be instrumental in collaborating with senior product owners, architects, compliance, and risk teams to deliver transformative solutions that meet the stringent demands of a global financial institution, ensuring seamless integration across the broader Microsoft ecosystem including Windows 365.

Responsibilities

* Define and execute the long-term strategic vision and roadmap for all Microsoft 365 platforms and services, ensuring alignment with overall firm objectives, technological advancements, and regulatory requirements.

* Lead, mentor, and develop a global team of highly skilled engineers and technical managers, fostering a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement.

* Provide executive oversight for the design, architecture, and implementation of complex, enterprise-grade Microsoft 365 solutions, ensuring scalability, security, resilience, and seamless integration with existing firm-wide systems, including Windows 365 and the Power Platform.

* Establish and enforce robust governance frameworks, security policies, and compliance standards for all Microsoft 365 services, ensuring adherence to internal policies and external regulatory mandates within a highly regulated financial services environment.

* Collaborate extensively with senior product owners, enterprise architects, engineering leads, compliance, risk, and business unit leaders to understand strategic requirements, manage expectations, and drive successful outcomes for critical initiatives.

* Drive the evaluation, pilot, and strategic adoption of new Microsoft 365 features, emerging technologies, and AI-powered capabilities (e.g., Copilot, Teams, Power Platform) to enhance productivity, collaboration, and user experience across the firm.

* Oversee the operational, performance, and incident management processes for Microsoft 365 environments, serving as a primary executive escalation point for critical issues and ensuring rapid resolution and root cause analysis.

Basic Qualifications

* 12-15+ years of progressive experience in enterprise-level IT, with at least 10 years specifically focused on Microsoft 365 platforms and services in a significant leadership capacity within a large, complex organization.

* Proven track record of successfully leading, managing, and developing large, geographically dispersed technical teams, including managers and individual contributors.

* Demonstrated ability to define, articulate, and execute complex technology strategies and

roadmaps for critical enterprise collaboration platforms.

* Deep architectural and engineering understanding of the entire Microsoft 365 suite, including Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Teams, OneDrive, Azure AD, Intune, Purview, Windows 365, and the Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI), as well as advanced security/compliance features.

* Extensive experience designing, implementing, and managing Microsoft 365 solutions within stringent security, governance, and regulatory frameworks, preferably in the financial services industry.

* Exceptional executive-level communication, presentation, and negotiation skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical concepts and strategic initiatives to diverse audiences, including senior leadership and non-technical stakeholders.

* Superior analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities, capable of navigating highly complex technical, organizational, and business challenges.

Preferred Qualifications

* Hands-on experience with large-scale Microsoft 365 Copilot deployments, Power Platform integrations, custom integrations, and advanced automation strategies for M365 management and operations.

* Strong understanding of broader cloud architecture principles (Azure, AWS, GCP) and experience managing hybrid cloud environments.

* Relevant industry certifications such as Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Microsoft 365 Certified: Enterprise Administrator Expert, or similar.

* Prior experience in a senior technology leadership role within a global financial institution, with a strong understanding of industry-specific challenges and regulations.

* Proficiency in advanced PowerShell scripting, Azure Automation, and other automation frameworks for M365 management and operations.

Salary Range The expected base salary for this Seattle, Washington, United States-based position is $150000-$250000. In addition, you may be eligible for a discretionary bonus if you are an active employee as of fiscal year-end.

Benefits Goldman Sachs is committed to providing our people with valuable and competitive benefits and wellness offerings, as it is a core part of providing a strong overall employee experience. A summary of these offerings, which are generally available to active, non-temporary, full-time and part-time US employees who work at least 20 hours per week, can be found here.

jobs.description:
Endpoint & Information Systems Specialist

POSITION SUMMARY:

The Endpoint & Information Systems Specialist designs, implements, and supports the district’s cloud, identity, and endpoint management systems across Microsoft 365, Azure, Google Workspace, and major device platforms. The role manages identity services, automation workflows, SIS integrations, and data accuracy while providing Level 3 technical support and ensuring compliance with state reporting requirements. This position requires strong technical expertise, problem‑solving skills, and the ability to support mission‑critical educational technology in a fast‑paced environment.

DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

jobs.title: Content Systems Engineer

jobs.description:
This technology team designs, builds, and supports critical systems used across OTV, Hulu, ABC News, Streaming, and other Disney Entertainment teams. As part of the ABC Owned Television Stations (ABC OTV) group within Disney Entertainment, we operate in a high-performance, innovative, and collaborative environment committed to quality, employee growth, and continuous improvement.We invest in our people—prioritizing work-life balance, ongoing training, and competitive compensation and benefits—because we believe technology is only as strong as the team behind it. Our mission is to empower our engineers and technologists to drive the future of media through creativity, expertise, and relentless innovation.

The Content Systems Engineer is a hands-on technical leader responsible for designing, implementing, and supporting mission-critical, cloud-native media systems across ABC OTV. This role drives the architecture and integration of scalable, secure, and resilient broadcast and content workflows, ensuring alignment with business, marketing, and entertainment objectives. The engineer collaborates
with cross-functional teams, manages third-party providers, and influences the strategic direction of next-generation broadcast and storage solutions. The position requires deep technical expertise, strong communication skills, and the ability to translate enterprise strategy into actionable system designs and roadmaps.

Responsibilities:

Architect, design, and optimize cloud-native and broadcast systemsDevelop and implement standards, policies, procedures, and documentationMonitor system health, troubleshoot issues, and ensure operational reliabilityCollaborate cross-functionally to enhance workflows and support business goalsManage resources, technology implementations, and third-party partners

Basic Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Broadcast Engineering, or equivalent experience3 years of broadcast or post-production engineering experienceAdvanced knowledge of media workflow and orchestration systems (e.g., Telestream Vantage, IBM Aspera, Dalet Galaxy)Strong experience with cloud platforms and services (e.g., AWS EC2, S3, Lambda, AWS media services)Proficiency in at least one programming or scripting language (e.g., Python, PowerShell)Experience with on-premises virtualization and storage solutions (e.g., VMware, NetApp Storage, Dell EMC Isilon)Familiarity with broadcast hardware (encoders, decoders, video routers, etc.)Understanding of live video transport protocols (SRT, RTMP, RTSP, NDI, etc.)Familiarity with post-production workflows (transcoding, archiving) and non-linear editing, VFX, and motion graphics software (Adobe Creative Suite, Maxon CINEMA 4D, DaVinci Resolve)Demonstrated ability to design and implement cloud-native and hybrid workflowsStrong analytical and troubleshooting skills, including Tier 2/3 supportExcellent organization and communication skills, with the ability to multitask and adapt to changing requirementsAbility to work early mornings, late nights, holidays, and weekends as needed for outages and/or maintenance windows, and participate in the department’s on-call rotation.

Preferred Qualification:

Project management experienceExperience negotiating with senior management and customers to influence strategic directionExperience developing and maintaining technical standards and reference architectures.

The hiring range for this position in Burbank, CA, Bristol, CT and Chicago, IL is 129,300.00-$173,300. The range in New York, NY and Seattle, WA is $135,382 - $181,610 . The range in San Francisco, CA is $141,532 - $189,860. The range in Orlando, FL is $123,082 - $165,110. The base pay actually offered will take into account internal equity and also may vary depending on the candidate’s geographic region, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience among other factors. A bonus and/or long-term incentive units may be provided as part of the compensation package, in addition to the full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits, dependent on the level and position offered.

jobs.title: IT - Sr Staff Systems Engineer (Active Directory, Powershell, and Windows/O365 Infrastructure Administration)
jobs.summary: At Cadence, we hire and develop leaders and innovators who want to make an impact on the world of technology. Job Summary: We are looking for a skilled detail-oriented specialist to manage and secure Cadence’s Active Directory environment, Windows server...
jobs.description:
At Cadence, we hire and develop leaders and innovators who want to make an impact on the world of technology.
Job Summary:

We are looking for a skilled detail-oriented specialist to manage and secure Cadence’s Active Directory environment, Windows server administration including OS hardening, vulnerability, patch management. Candidate with solid background in Windows based environment and Active Directory architecture, identity, and access management, and securing directory services against evolving threats.

Job Description:

Provide day-to-day administration and support of Microsoft Active Directory environments (version 2016/209 and above).

Version upgrade of Windows Active Directory. Setting up Read/Write and Read only domain controllers.

Maintain and troubleshoot Group Policy Objects (GPOs) to ensure secure and consistent configurations.

Setup and manager authentication Policies / Silos, gMSA.

Work with DNS, DHCP, and other core services closely integrated with AD.

Perform regular audits and cleanup of AD objects to maintain a secure and efficient environment.

Monitor and troubleshoot authentication issues including Kerberos, NTLM, and account lockouts.

Ensure the security and integrity of the Active Directory infrastructure.

Conduct regular audits of AD objects, group memberships, and permissions to identify and remediate misconfigurations or security risks.

Administer and maintain Windows Server environments. Implement and maintain security hardening across Windows Servers. Version 2016/1029 and above

Analyze security bulletins, prioritize patches based on risk, and deploy patches.

Document changes, configurations, and standard operating procedures.

Proficiency in PowerShell or other scripting languages for task automation.

Requirements:

10+ years of experience in support of Active directory and windows server environments.

Proficiency with PowerShell scripting and other scripting tools for auditing, automation, and remediation.

In-depth knowledge of Active Directory, Kerberos, GPOs, DNS, and DHCP.

Strong understanding of Microsoft Windows Server environments and administration.

Experience with a Microsoft O365 environment is a plus.

Familiarity with security best practices for hybrid identity environments.

Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills.

Good written and verbal Communications Skills.

Education:

Must have a Bachelors Degree in Engineering/Technical field.

Job Location:

This is an onsite position. Must be able to work in-person from Cadence’s San Jose office; Monday through Thursday.

The annual salary range for California is $136,500 to $253,500. You may also be eligible to receive incentive compensation: bonus, equity, and benefits. Sales positions generally offer a competitive On Target Earnings (OTE) incentive compensation structure. Please note that the salary range is a guideline and compensation may vary based on factors such as qualifications, skill level, competencies and work location. Our benefits programs include: paid vacation and paid holidays, 401(k) plan with employer match, employee stock purchase plan, a variety of medical, dental and vision plan options, and more.

We’re doing work that matters. Help us solve what others can’t.

jobs.title: Senior MDM Engineer
jobs.summary: At F5, we strive to bring a better digital world to life. Our teams empower organizations across the globe to create, secure, and run applications that enhance how we experience our evolving digital world. We are passionate about cybersecurity, from...
jobs.description:
At F5, we strive to bring a better digital world to life. Our teams empower organizations across the globe to create, secure, and run applications that enhance how we experience our evolving digital world. We are passionate about cybersecurity, from protecting consumers from fraud to enabling companies to focus on innovation.

Everything we do centers around people. That means we obsess over how to make the lives of our customers, and their customers, better. And it means we prioritize a diverse F5 community where each individual can thrive.

Job Title: Senior MDM Engineer

Overview

The Senior MDM Engineer is responsible for leading the design, implementation, and management of client device solutions across the organization. This pivotal role requires advanced expertise in mobile device management (MDM) platforms, particularly JAMF and Intune, as well as a strong background in solution architecture and scripting. The Senior Client Engineer works collaboratively with technical teams and leadership, providing guidance, mentoring, and strategic insights to ensure robust, scalable, and secure client environments. The role also demands extensive experience in Device Trust frameworks, Azure cloud services, and conditional access policies, enabling secure and compliant device connectivity throughout the organization. Integration of AI and heavy automation is a key aspect, driving innovation and efficiency in device management and security.

Key Responsibilities

Design, deploy, and maintain MDM solutions, with a primary focus on JAMF and Intune, to ensure optimal device management and security. Develop and implement client device architectures that align with organizational requirements and best practices, including Device Trust frameworks to enforce secure endpoints. Create, optimize, and maintain advanced scripts to automate device management tasks and improve operational efficiency, leveraging AI-driven automation where applicable. Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to deliver integrated solutions and resolve complex technical challenges, including the implementation of Azure-based conditional access controls. Provide guidance, coaching, and technical mentorship to engineering team members, fostering professional growth and knowledge sharing. Partner with leadership to shape team strategy, set priorities, and contribute to the continuous improvement of client engineering practices, with a focus on expanding automation and AI capabilities. Manage multiple projects and workloads effectively, ensuring timely delivery and high-quality outcomes.

Qualifications

Extensive hands-on experience with MDM solutions, especially JAMF and Intune. Extensive knowledge of macOS, Windows and Mobile OS’s Proven expertise in solution architecture for Client environments. Advanced proficiency in scripting languages such as PowerShell, Bash, or Python, including experience developing AI-powered automation scripts. Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly to diverse audiences. Demonstrated experience guiding and mentoring technical teams. Extensive experience implementing and managing Device Trust solutions, including the use of certificates and secure device identity verification. In-depth expertise in Microsoft Azure, with a focus on configuring and maintaining conditional access policies to safeguard organizational endpoints. Strong background in developing and deploying automation solutions, leveraging AI technologies to enhance device management, compliance, and security operations.

The Job Description is intended to be a general representation of the responsibilities and requirements of the job. However, the description may not be all-inclusive, and responsibilities and requirements are subject to change.

The annual base pay for this position is: $138,400.00 - $207,600.00

F5 maintains broad salary ranges for its roles in order to account for variations in knowledge, skills, experience, geographic locations, and market conditions, as well as to reflect F5’s differing products, industries, and lines of business. The pay range referenced is as of the time of the job posting and is subject to change.

You may also be offered incentive compensation, bonus, restricted stock units, and benefits. More details about F5’s benefits can be found at the following link: https://www.f5.com/company/careers/benefits. F5 reserves the right to change or terminate any benefit plan without notice.

Please note that F5 only contacts candidates through F5 email address (ending with @f5.com) or auto email notification from Workday (ending with f5.com or @myworkday.com).

Equal Employment Opportunity

It is the policy of F5 to provide equal employment opportunities to all employees and employment applicants without regard to unlawful considerations of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, sensory, physical, or mental disability, marital status, veteran or military status, genetic information, or any other classification protected by applicable local, state, or federal laws. This policy applies to all aspects of employment, including, but not limited to, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, discipline, and termination. F5 offers a variety of reasonable accommodations for candidates. Requesting an accommodation is completely voluntary. F5 will assess the need for accommodations in the application process separately from those that may be needed to perform the job. Request by contacting accommodations@f5.com.

jobs.title: Endpoint Administrator
jobs.summary: About Us A Fortune 500 company, EMCOR Group, Inc. (NYSE: EME) is a leader in mechanical and electrical construction, industrial and energy infrastructure, and building services. EMCOR companies plan, install, operate, maintain, and protect the...
jobs.description:
About Us

A Fortune 500 company, EMCOR Group, Inc. (NYSE: EME) is a leader in mechanical and electrical construction, industrial and energy infrastructure, and building services. EMCOR companies plan, install, operate, maintain, and protect the sophisticated and dynamic systems that create facility environments-such as electrical, mechanical, lighting, air conditioning, heating, security, fire protection, and power generation systems-in virtually every sector of the economy and for a diverse range of businesses, organizations, and government.

Job SummaryEMCOR Group, Inc. seeks an Endpoint Administrator for a critical role responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining enterprise-level endpoint solutions to ensure security, performance, scalability, and seamless operations. This position focuses on managing and resolving complex IT tickets from our network of over 150 IT professionals while collaborating with local IT teams and other stakeholders across EMCOR's 100 operating companies. The Endpoint Administrator will leverage Microsoft Intune for devise management across Windows, iOS, and Android platforms, performing basic design and advanced troubleshooting to support our business objectives.

Essential Duties & Responsibilities Design, test, validate, and deploy enterprise-scale endpoint solutions, including device configurations, OS deployments, and software distribution using Microsoft Intune. Triage and resolve complex IT tickets escalated from operating company IT teams, ensuring timely and effective solutions. Create and maintain standardized configurations for desktops, laptops, and mobile devices, ensuring compatibility with existing and new hardware. Document endpoint environments, configurations, deployments, and changes to maintain a clear and accurate knowledge base. Support pilot programs to evaluate new technologies and ensure compatibility with existing infrastructure. Provide escalated support and troubleshooting for complex endpoint issues, serving as a subject matter expert for endpoint management. Automate routing tasks using scripting languages (e.g., PowerShell, PSADT) to improve efficiency. Package and deploy Win32 applications via Intune and Patch My PC, ensuring seamless software distribution. Stay current with industry trends by participating in conferences, workshops, and training. Conduct regular knowledge-sharing sessions with internal teams to promote best practices and new technologies while fostering innovation and collaboration across the organization.Qualifications#emcor

Minimum of 3 years of experience managing Windows devices with Microsoft Intune, including MAM/MDM administration. Minimum of 3 years of experience with imaging and Windows Autopilot provisioning. Associate's degree in IT, Computer Science, or a related field (preferred); equivalent experience considered. Expertise in operating systems, including Windows 11, iOS, macOS, and Android. Advanced knowledge of Apple Business Manager (Automated Device Enrollment, VPP applications) and Android Zero-touch enrollment. Proficiency in scripting languages (e.g., PowerShell, PSADT) for task automation. Experience packaging and deploying Win32 applications via Intune and Patch My PC. Strong problem solving, decision making, and troubleshooting skills. Self-motivated with the ability to work independently or collaboratively in a team environment. Excellent communication skills to interact effectively with personnel at all levels. Commitment to delivering exceptional customer service and maintaining positive interpersonal relationships.Equal Opportunity EmployerAs a leading provider of mechanical and electrical construction, facilities services, and energy infrastructure, we offer employees a competitive salary and benefits package and we are always looking for individuals with the talent and skills required to contribute to our continued growth and success. Equal Opportunity Employer/Veterans/DisabledAffirmative Action PolicyPlease review our Affirmative Action Policy.

Notice to Prospective EmployeesNotice to prospective employees: There have been fraudulent postings and emails regarding job openings. EMCOR Group and its companies list open positions here. Please check our available positions to confirm that a post or email is genuine. EMCOR Group and its companies do not reach out to individuals to help with marketing or other similar services. If an individual is contacted for services outside of EMCOR's normal application process - it is probably fraudulent.Employment Type: FULL_TIME

Are you looking to power the next leap in the exciting world of advanced electronics? Do you want to help solve problems that drive success in the rapidly evolving technology and connectivity landscape? Then bring your problem-solving, passion, and creativity to help us power the next leap in electronics.

At Qnity, we’re more than a global leader in materials and solutions for advanced electronics and high-tech industries – we’re a tight-knit team that is motivated by new possibilities, and always up for a challenge. All our dedicated teams contribute to making cutting-edge technology possible. We value forward-thinking challengers, boundary-pushers, and diverse perspectives across all our departments, because we know we play a critical role in the world enabling faster progress for all. Learn how you can start or jumpstart your career with us.

We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated End User Computing (EUC) Engineer to support and enhance our enterprise end-user environment. This role is responsible for the design, deployment, maintenance, and support of end-user devices, operating systems, and productivity tools, ensuring a seamless and secure user experience across the organization.

The ideal candidate has strong expertise in both hardware lifecycle management (laptops, desktops, peripherals) and software deployment and support (OS, applications, endpoint management platforms).

Install, configure, and support enterprise applications including Microsoft 365Troubleshoot OS-level and application issuesSupport software packaging and distributionEnsure compatibility and performance across devices

Security & Compliance

Enforce endpoint security standardsSupport vulnerability remediation efforts (e.g., CVEs, patch validation)Ensure devices meet corporate security and compliance policies

User Support & Experience

Provide Tier 2/3 escalation support for complex user issuesAnalyze trends and proactively improve user experienceDevelop user documentation and support processesPartner with Service Desk teams to improve resolution efficiency

Automation & Continuous Improvement

Develop scripts to automate administrative tasksIdentify and implement process improvementsSupport migration initiatives

Strong troubleshooting and root cause analysis skillsAbility to work across both hardware and software domainsExcellent communication and documentation skillsCustomer-focused mindset with attention to detailAbility to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environmentTypical Tools & Technologies

jobs.summary: Overview The Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer is responsible for overseeing the organization's end-user computing environment, ensuring the efficient operation, security, and optimization of desktop, laptop, and virtual desktop infrastructure...
jobs.description:
Overview

The Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer is responsible for overseeing the organization's end-user computing environment, ensuring the efficient operation, security, and optimization of desktop, laptop, and virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI). This role involves advanced troubleshooting, leadership in project management, and collaborating with IT teams on technology strategy and integration. The Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer will act as a key technical expert in designing and implementing complex desktop solutions across the enterprise. Pay range for the role is $140,000 to $150,000, with an annual bonus. The role can be 100% remote anywhere in United States.

Responsibilities

Advanced Desktop Infrastructure Management:Architect, deploy and maintain the organization's desktop environments, including Windows-based physical and virtual desktops (VDI).Develop and manage automated desktop deployments using tools such as Microsoft System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) or equivalent technologies.Design, implement, and maintain desktop images, ensuring they are optimized for performance and security.Leadership and Strategy:Lead desktop infrastructure projects, such as OS migrations, VDI implementations, and major software rollouts.Provide mentorship and technical guidance to junior engineers and support staff.Collaborate with senior IT leadership to develop and implement long-term desktop technology strategies.Security and Compliance:Ensure compliance with internal security policies, industry standards, and audit requirements.Lead initiatives to enhance desktop security, including endpoint protection, encryption, and vulnerability management.Manage group policies, endpoint security software, and patch management processes using WSUS or other tools.Troubleshooting and Escalation:Serve as the highest escalation point for complex desktop-related issues, providing Level 3 support.Perform in-depth root cause analysis for recurring or complex technical problems and implement preventative measures.Provide expert support for remote access solutions, VPNs, and cloud desktop services.Automation and Efficiency:Develop and implement automation scripts using PowerShell and other tools to streamline desktop management tasks.Continuously seek opportunities to improve end-user computing efficiency, including desktop performance tuning and reducing downtime.System Integration:Collaborate with server, network, and security teams to ensure the seamless integration of desktop systems into the broader IT infrastructure.Ensure optimal configuration and performance of desktop environments within cloud services like Microsoft 365 and Azure Active Directory.Manage and integrate virtualization technologies (e.g., VMware Horizon, Citrix, Hyper-V).Documentation and Knowledge Sharing:Develop and maintain comprehensive documentation for desktop systems, policies, and procedures.Ensure knowledge sharing and training for IT staff on new technologies, best practices, and troubleshooting techniques.Actively participate in developing and updating standard operating procedures (SOPs) for desktop support.
Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (or equivalent experience).5+ years of experience in desktop engineering or support, with significant experience in enterprise-level desktop management.Demonstrated experience leading major desktop infrastructure projects, including large-scale deployments, migrations, and VDI implementation.Expertise in Microsoft technologies, including SCCM, Intune, and Active Directory.Advanced knowledge of Windows 10/11, Group Policy, and desktop management tools.Strong PowerShell scripting skills for automation and troubleshooting.Experience with virtualization technologies (e.g., VMware, Citrix, Hyper-V) and virtual desktop environments (VDI).Familiarity with cloud-based services and integration (Microsoft 365, Azure AD, etc.).Strong leadership and team collaboration skills.Ability to manage multiple priorities and projects in a fast-paced environment.Excellent problem-solving and critical thinking abilities.Outstanding communication skills, both written and verbal.Certifications, Licenses, and/or RegistrationMicrosoft Certified: Modern Desktop Administrator ExpertMicrosoft Certified: Azure Fundamentals or Administrator AssociateVMware VCP or Citrix certifications.ITIL Certification or other relevant service management certifications.Physical Demands and Work EnvironmentThe physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit and use hands to handle, touch or feel objects, tools, or controls. The employee frequently is required to talk and hear. The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate. The employee is occasionally required to stand; walk; reach with hands and arms. The employee is rarely required to stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl. The employee must regularly lift and/or move up to 10 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, color vision, and the ability to adjust focus.

All aspects of consideration for employment and employment with the Company are governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other category protected by federal, state, or local law.

jobs.summary: About INflow Federal - founded in 2013, INflow Federal is a mission-driven small business delivering cutting-edge solutions to the Department of War (DoW) and Joint Force operations across 20+ states. Our strength comes from our people - especially the...
jobs.description:
About INflow Federal - founded in 2013, INflow Federal is a mission-driven small business delivering cutting-edge solutions to the Department of War (DoW) and Joint Force operations across 20+ states. Our strength comes from our people - especially the Veterans who make up over 50% of our workforce. Through our Veteran Outreach Program and employee-first culture, we invest deeply in professional growth, well-being, and innovation. Known for our agility, transparency, and integrity, INflow combines real-world experience with emerging technologies like AI/ML to help our customers lead in a rapidly evolving defense landscape. We empower both our employees and mission partners to stay ahead - driving smarter, faster, and more secure outcomes.
Are you passionate about engineering cutting-edge solutions and optimizing system performance? INflow Federal is seeking a dedicated systems engineer to provide Windows desktop engineering and systems administration at enterprise scale, in a classified environment. The core of the job is designing, building, packaging, deploying, and maintaining standardized Windows desktop images across a large, geographically distributed network—both physical machines and virtual desktops. If you are ready to take on challenging projects, collaborate with top-tier engineers, and contribute to the future of technology, we invite you to apply and be part of our mission to empower federal agencies with powerful, integrated solutions. Here, your work is more than a job—it's a journey in innovation. With opportunities to work on high-impact projects, access to the latest technologies, and a culture that thrives on creativity and collaboration, INflow Federal is where your expertise can truly make a difference.Specific Duties and Responsibilities:Designs and defines system architecture for Windows desktop solutions and client- based applicationsSupports the integration of the Windows desktop with a variety of enterprise servicesEvaluates desktop software and hardware technologies and determines the most efficient method to leverage the technology within the customer’s network environment.Administer, operate, manage and maintain components for Windows desktop deployments to include virtual and thick client environments.Reviews completion and implementation of system additions and/or enhancements and makes recommendations to management and/or clientsPlans and directs upgrades of Windows desktop operating system version(s) and supporting infrastructure and related components.Required Skills:CompTIA Security+ or similar for IAT Level II DoD 8570 certification.Active TS/SCI and CI PolygraphMECM / MCM packages experience as well as vast knowledge in OSD (O/S Deployment), MDT (Microsoft deployment toolkit) and Intune.Experience with Microsoft Windows desktop design engineering and implementation options.Experience with Microsoft deployment tools to include Microsoft Deployment Toolkit and Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager.Experience with engineering and deploying client software installation packages for Microsoft Windows desktops (physical and
